16-07-26 Gwen is a gorgeous little puppy who came into our care as an unsold pup. She is full of life, loves a cuddle, and will make a wonderful addition to a loving home. She is incredibly easy to handle, adores affection, and is an absolute delight to be around.

Gwen has come into our care with two open fontanelles on the right side of her skull. These are currently being monitored by our veterinary team. While some puppies' fontanelles close as they mature, others remain open. If they do remain open, extra care may be needed to protect the area from injury, and regular veterinary monitoring is recommended. As this is a pre-existing condition, it may not be covered by pet insurance. Despite this, Gwen is a happy, playful, and affectionate little puppy who doesn't let it stop her enjoying life.

As Gwen is still just a puppy, she will need patient adopters who are committed to teaching her all about home life. She will need help with the basics, including house training and learning to walk on a lead once she is fully vaccinated and has been given the all-clear by our veterinary team.

Gwen could be an only dog or she could live with smaller sized, kind and gentle resident dogs who will be careful around her and don't mind sharing their space. She could also live with dog-savvy cats and sensible, gentle teenagers aged 13+ who understand that she is still a young puppy.

Gwen's adopters will need to agree to have her spayed when she is old enough and commit to taking her to puppy socialisation and training classes to help her grow into a confident, well-rounded adult dog. Gwen is a truly special little girl who is sure to bring so much love and happiness to her forever family.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Buck
description:
I would like an active home filled with love and kindness!

I can be an only dog or live with a calm female dog.

I would love a garden to run and play in!

I can walk well on a harness and would love an active home.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I have no tail, but this doesn't bother me!

Please meet the very handsome Buck, who has travelled all the way from Romania in the hope of finding his forever home. Since arriving here a few months ago, Buck has truly blossomed! Initially a little shy with new people, he now settles quickly and happily shows off his affectionate, gentle nature. He loves attention, enjoys fuss, and is more than happy to make new friends once he feels comfortable.

Buck is such a comical boy, always finding little ways to make you smile, but he is also incredibly affectionate. He has that spark of energy that brightens even the hardest of days.

Buck absolutely loves his food. You’ll hear his excited bark echo around the rescue at mealtimes without fail. He also really enjoys his walks and having a good run around the big yard, where he can let out all that happy energy. Buck also has the most cuddly side to him; he loves nothing more than resting his head on your lap while he slowly dozes off to sleep.

Buck was briefly adopted but was sadly returned as he didn't get along with the resident dog. Buck enjoys the company of well mannered female does and does enjoy sharing his kennel with another dog, so we would consider homing him with a laid back female dog if they were the right match. He can get a little jealous of sharing attention with another dog, but in the right home with experienced adopters this could be managed. That said, Buck would be equally happy to be homed as the only dog and get all the love and attention for himself.

Buck will be looking for an adult only home and would be best suited to adopters who have experience of owning dogs from a similar background before and who are around most of the time, so they can spend lots of time with him. He would love to find an active home where he can be kept both physically and mentally stimulated and enjoy plenty of walks and adventures with his new family.

Although Buck had never lived in a home before being adopted, he settled in wonderfully and was clean in the house. He wasn’t phased by the TV or radio and quickly found the sofas and beds! He can still be a little shy when first meeting men, but it doesn’t take him long to warm up. Buck also isn’t keen on sudden loud noises or sticks and brooms, so this is something his adopters will need to work on desensitising him to. He doesn’t like having his front paws touched, but he will politely let you know by gently pulling them away.

Buck is a truly special boy with so much love to give. He is absolutely wasted in kennels and will make a loyal, devoted companion for a very lucky family. He arrived with no tail, but this doesn’t bother him in the slightest and only adds to his charm. Please don’t overlook this wonderful lad, as Buck is more than ready to find his forever home.

Buck has received negative test results for Brucellosis, Leishmania, Giardia, Heartworm, Ehrlichia canis, Borrelia burgdorferi and Anaplasma.

Buck will need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

Buck has now been with us for 7 months and is still patiently waiting for his forever home.

Buck absolutely loves the people he knows and trusts. If Buck is unsure of someone, it will take him awhile to come out of his shell and get to know them, but once they've built a bond with him, he is the most loving gentle boy, who loves attention!

Buck has plenty of doggy friends and enjoys socialising outside of his kennel environment, although he definitely has a preference for the ladies and gets on best with female dogs.

Buck can potentially live with another calm female dog who will respect his space when he needs it.

Buck is looking for an adopters who will give him the time he needs to settle and understand that he can be a very worried boy at first.

I do genuinely love Buck with my entire heart. I would love nothing more than seeing him finally settling into home life, with people who will cherish him just as much as I do!

For the past few weeks, Buck has been front and centre in our big yard play group! This is a great chance for our people-shy new dogs to meet and play with more confident long-stayers like Buck, and he's met a lot of new dogs this way, which suggests that once he's settled into his new home, he might benefit from a dog park or opportunities to meet up with friends.

Buck's best friend is another Romanian dog called Olive. You rarely see one without the other, in their yard, the big yard, or on walks! Sometimes in big yard play group, Bucky and Olive will separate from the other dogs and chase and wrestle each other for a while, and it's lovely to see. Unfortunately, Buck and Olive don't like to be kenneled together as they prefer their own space to unwind after all that time spent playing.

However, Buck has really come out of his shell with people around the rescue. He has a few people he's very close to, and is always up for cwtches with them. How can you say no that handsome face? He's also happier now to meet new people than he used to be, and now goes on walks with volunteers (accompanied, of course, by Olive) most days.

Buck can still get possessive over his favourite people, which is something we have to be aware of in big yard play group and that his adopters will need to bear in mind. He may grumble at other dogs in warning if they get too close when he's having his cuddles, though this does not usually escalate beyond a warning snap. It's likely that our sweet Bucky may not have had stable affection in his past, so he feels the need to guard that love from other dogs.

Luckily for Buck, we have plenty of love to go around at Many Tears! Hopefully, as he settles, he'll realise he is safe and loved and doesn't have to guard anyone anymore.

I’ve had the absolute pleasure of working with Buck, or “Buckaroo” as I like to call him, since the very day he arrived with us. I know you’re not supposed to have favourites, but if I’m honest, Buck has completely stolen a place in my heart.

Buck is such a comical boy, always finding little ways to make you smile, but he is also incredibly affectionate. He has that spark of energy that can turn even the hardest days into better ones.

Buck’s kennel friend, who he came over from Romania with, has recently been adopted. He adored her so much, and since she left, there’s been a little piece of him missing. Now he is the one still waiting, and it is heartbreaking knowing he doesn’t understand why it is not his turn yet.

Although Buck has shared his kennel and enjoys playdates with other dogs, always greeting them nicely out on walks, he would also thrive as the only dog in a home. He loves human attention so deeply and would really benefit from having people around most of the time to give him the love, comfort, and reassurance he deserves.

I recently took Buck out for a day to a local beach. From the moment we arrived, he was in his absolute element and enjoyed every second of it. Buck spent the majority of the time on a long line; although he ran around and explored, he also came back to me when called and always turned to make sure I was still close. This just shows how much of a loyal companion he can be.

I honestly love Buck with my whole heart, and it breaks me a little every single day knowing he is still waiting for his people to come for him. He has so much love, loyalty, and joy to give; he just needs someone to give him that chance.

It’s been a few weeks since Bucks arrival, and oh my has he made his presence known. Every single staff member who has had the pleasure of meeting this boy is utterly obsessed, and it’s very clear as to why!

Buck’s energy is infectious, he is the happiest, bounciest, most adoring little man and whoever adopts him will be truly lucky!!! When you meet him you’d never assume he is 6 years old - he exudes puppy energy.

Buck loves to play with his fellow Romanian buddy Jenny and they get along great. He also goes for play dates with the lovely Orietta, and he thoroughly enjoys it. He would like adopters who have experience with owning dogs before and who are around most of the time.

This sweet sweet boy is absolutely wasted in kennels, he is bursting with love to give and he’ll make a very lucky someone the happiest person in the world. Our foreign dogs are often overlooked and tend to hang around in kennels much longer than needed - please don’t let this be the case for our lovely Buck.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Archie
description:
I'd like a quiet, adult-only home to help me relax and feel safe.

I’d like to be the only dog in the home. I haven’t met cats

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I'm new to lead walking and need some practice!

I have lived in a home so I know the basics.

I have been neutered and have had 21 teeth removed.

07-07-26 Archie is such a sweet little man who came into our care from a breeder. He was adopted but has sadly been returned after his adopters felt they were not the right match for him.

Archie formed a very strong bond with his female owner, but found it much harder to build a relationship with her husband, who was frequently away from home and therefore had very little opportunity to gain Archie's trust. When Archie becomes closely bonded to his favourite person, he can become protective of them. In his previous home, he nipped when he was sitting with his owner and her husband approached, so his new adopters will need to understand this behaviour and be prepared to continue building his confidence in a positive, patient way.

Archie has settled back into rescue life well and is currently sharing his kennel with a lovely female dog, who he gets on very well with. He is a cuddly, affectionate little dog once he feels comfortable, although he isn't quite brave enough to take treats from our hands just yet.

Although Archie gets on well with other dogs, he can become protective of his favourite person around them, so we think he would be happiest as the only dog in the home, where he can relax and build a close bond with his new family.

As Archie has lived in a home before, he will already be familiar with the sights and sounds of home life. However, he may need a little reminding of his house training in a new environment, and he would benefit from some more practice walking on a lead.

Archie is looking for a quiet, adult-only home with adopters who have experience of the breed and of rescue dogs. He will need people who are happy to give him the time and space he needs to settle and understand that he is safe.

Archie is doing really well since returning to us and his confidence is growing all the time. He is such a loving little dog, and in the right home, we hope he will continue to thrive and become a devoted companion.

name: Gloria
description:
I’d like a home filled with love and kindness!

I can be an only dog or live with other dogs. I have never met cats.

I will need a very secure garden with minimum 6ft, non climbable fencing.

I can walk on a lead and will enjoy going on adventures with my new family!

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I will be spayed before finding my forever home.

16-07-26 Please meet Gloria! Gloria has come to us from a breeder and shortly after arriving, we discovered she was pregnant. She went on to have seven beautiful puppies and has been the most wonderful mum to them all. Now that her puppies are old enough to find their own forever homes, it's finally Gloria's turn to find hers.

Gloria is a lovely, affectionate girl who absolutely loves being with people. She has become very bonded to her carers here and is always happiest when she's by their side. We think she is going to thrive in a home where she can enjoy lots of love, attention and adventures with her very own family.

She is confident enough, that we would consider homing her as an only dog in the right circumstances, but she would also be very happy to live with another kind and friendly resident dog.As Gloria has become so attached to her carers, we do feel she could struggle with being left initially and may develop some separation anxiety. Because of this, she will need adopters who are around for most of the day and who are willing to build up the time she is left gradually, helping her to settle in at her own pace. We would consider homing her with dog savvy teenagers.

Gloria has never lived in a home before, so her adopters will need to be kind and patient as she settles in and learns all about home life and new skills like house training. Shewill need a very secure garden with non-climbable fencing of at least 6ft as she has shown us that she can be a bit of a Dora the Explorer when she's trying to find her favourite people! She has never attempted to escape when she's with her carers, but she is very determined to find them if left on her own, so secure boundaries are essential.

She is a busy girl who would love an active home where she can enjoy plenty of walks and continue exploring the world. She has done brilliantly learning to walk on a lead with us and really enjoys getting out and about.

Gloria has spent the last few months doing an amazing job raising her puppies. Now it's her time to be loved, cherished and spoiled, and we can't wait to see her enjoying the happy life she so deserves.

Gloria has been given the Gift of Life by Hannah West

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Chewwie
description:
I’d like a quiet home filled with love and kindness!

I would like a home with another dog medium size or larger. I am fine with cats.

I will need a secure garden with minimum 6ft fencing.

I'm new to lead walking and need some practice!

I have lived in a home so I know the basics.

I have been neutered and have had 2 teeth removed!

Chewwie originally came to us from a breeder to find his forever home. He was lucky enough to be adopted, but sadly, despite being very loved, it became clear that the home wasn't quite the right match for him and he has now returned to our care to begin his search once again. Chewwie was very worried of the adult sons in the home and also wasn't a great match with the smaller resident dog.

Chewwie is a sensitive boy who had experienced very little before coming into rescue and can find unfamiliar situations overwhelming. In his previous home, he formed a very close bond with his female adopter and absolutely adored her, but remained worried around the adult sons in the household. For this reason, he is looking for a calm and quiet home with patient adopters who have experience of nervous ex-breeding dogs and who will allow him all the time he needs to settle and build trust.

Since returning to our care, however, Chewwie has been an absolute pleasure. From the day he came back, he has been nothing but gentle, loving and affectionate with everyone here. Despite being returned because he was said to be nervous, we have seen a completely different side to him in kennels. He happily bounces over to greet us, is always looking for a fuss, and simply loves being around people. His affectionate nature shines through every day, and he has reminded us just what a special boy he is.

Although he had never lived in a home before his adoption, Chewwie adapted beautifully to family life. He was house trained and thoroughly enjoyed all the comforts of home, especially curling up in his cosy beds. He did find being left alone quite worrying, so his new adopters will need to be around for most of the time initially and gradually build up any time spent alone as his confidence grows.

Chewwie will need another kind and confident resident dog in his new home to help reassure him and show him the ropes, but this dog will need to be medium-sized or larger. He previously shared his home with a cat and happily ignored them, so he could potentially live with a dog-savvy cat.

Since being adopted, Chewwie has learnt to walk nicely on a lead and now really enjoys his walks, although he can still be worried in unfamiliar places and will benefit from ongoing encouragement as his confidence continues to grow.

Once Chewwie knows you, he is an incredibly loving and affectionate companion with a wonderfully cheeky personality. He loves playing with other dogs and is especially fond of his kennel friend. He's also a total water baby and absolutely loves splashing around in the paddling pool, making the most of every opportunity for fun. Like many mischievous youngsters, he has also been known to pinch things he shouldn't! His adopters will need to keep a close eye on shoes and other tempting items, as he developed quite a fondness for chewing shoes in his previous home. He especially enjoys hard toys, while softer toys and enrichment items should be supervised as he can quickly chew through them.

Chewwie is a truly special boy who has so much love to give. His return to rescue has shown us just how affectionate, gentle and resilient he is, and we know that in the right home he will continue to flourish. In return, his lucky adopters will gain a devoted, fun-loving and wonderfully cheeky companion who will fill their days with smiles.

When Chewwie first arrived, he had a lick granuloma on his front leg, which healed well. Following his return to the rescue, he began limping on the same leg and holding it up. X-rays at a local veterinary practice showed no abnormalities or injuries, so it is thought to be a soft tissue injury which should continue to improve. His adopters and their own vet should continue to monitor this as a precaution.

Chewwie has been given the Gift of Life by Paula Elliot

Chewwie is an absolute angel. From the day he arrived on my section, he's been nothing but gentle, loving, affectionate, and just a truly special soul.

Despite being returned because he was said to be nervous, I honestly haven't seen that side of him. With me, he's all about cuddles and attention. He'll happily bounce over to say hello and is always looking for affection. He's also got a cheeky side that keeps me smiling. If you leave your folder, phone, or radio lying around for even a second, don't be surprised if Chewwie decides it's his now!

He's a total water baby and absolutely loves splashing around in the pool with his kennel friend. Watching him let loose, have fun, and just be a dog is one of my favourite things. He also loves heading down to the big yard where he can have a good sniff, stretch his legs, and enjoy himself.

Chewwie really is such an amazing dog, and I feel so lucky to know him. Looking after him has been an absolute privilege.

Chewwie is a sensitive and nervous boy who had experienced very little before coming into rescue and still finds new situations quite overwhelming. In his previous home, he formed a very close bond with his female adopter and absolutely adored her, but he remained worried around men and struggled to relax around the adult sons in the household. He will therefore be looking for a calm and quiet home with patient adopters who have experience of scared ex-breeding dogs and who will allow him all the time he needs to settle and build trust.

Although he had never lived in a home before his adoption, Chewwie adapted beautifully to home life. He was house trained and thoroughly enjoyed all the comforts that a home has to offer, especially his cosy beds! He did find being left alone quite worrying, so his new adopters will need to be around for most of the time initially and slowly build up the amount of time he is left as his confidence grows.

Chewwie will need another kind and confident resident dog in his new home to help reassure him and show him the ropes, but this dog will need to be medium size or larger. He previously shared his home with a cat and happily ignored them, so he could potentially live with a dog savvy cat.

Since being adopted, Chewwie has also learnt how to walk on a lead and now enjoys his walks, although he can still be worried in unfamiliar places and will need ongoing support and encouragement as he continues to gain confidence.

Once Chewwie gets to know you, he is an incredibly loving and affectionate boy who adores a fuss and cuddle. He has a wonderfully cheeky personality and loves to play with other dogs. He is also a bit of a mischievous boy and has been known to pinch things he shouldn't! His adopters will need to keep a close eye on shoes and other tempting items, as we are told he developed quite a fondness for chewing shoes in his previous home. He especially loves hard toys, but softer toys and enrichment items will need to be supervised as he can quickly chew through them.

Chewwie is a truly special boy who has so much love to give. In the right home he will start to come out of his shell and truly flourish. In return, his lucky adopters will gain a devoted, affectionate and wonderfully cheeky friend who will make them smile every day.

When Chewwie first arrived he had a lick granuloma on his front leg, which healed well. When he returned to the rescue he was limping on his front leg and holding it up, we took him to a local vets for an x-ray and no abnormalities or injuries were found, so it's possible that it is a soft tissue injury which should start to feel better soon, but this is something his adopters and their vet will need to monitor.

name: Sybil
description:
I would like a home with adopters who have experience with high energy breeds.

I can be an only dog or live with other dogs. I have never met cats.

I would love a garden to run and play in!

I am just a puppy so I need to learn to walk on a lead.

Like all puppies I will need to learn house training.

My adopter will need to agree to have me spayed when I'm old enough.

15-07-26 Sybil is a young puppy who has come to us from a home after her family found they were struggling to cope with her. She is an incredibly bright, fun loving girl with so much potential, and we have no doubt that in the right home she is going to absolutely thrive.

Sybil is a very clever, high energy puppy who we feel missed out on some important socialisation as a young puppy. Combined with her intelligence and enthusiasm for life, this has resulted in a puppy who can become very overexcited and doesn't always know the appropriate way to channel her excitement. In her previous home she would often chase the children and nip their ankles as well as become very mouthy during play. She is now looking for a home that can provide kind, consistent guidance and help her learn the skills she needs to grow into a well-rounded adult dog.

She isn't suited for a first time dog owner and will be looking for adopters who understand the needs of busy, intelligent puppies and are committed to positive reward-based training with clear, consistent boundaries. Sybil loves to play but can become so excited that she directs her mouth towards whatever is closest. Her new family will need to be proactive in redirecting her onto suitable toys, making them exciting and rewarding so she learns what is appropriate to chew and play with.

Sybil will need a home with adopters who have experience of energetic breeds and who are prepared to provide her with plenty of mental stimulation. Once she's old enough she will also need plenty of exercise too. At the moment we would only consider homing her with teenagers aged 16+.

Sybil is already showing us just how clever she is. She already knows "sit" and has begun learning both "touch" and "lay down", picking things up very quickly. Once she's had the chance to burn off some energy, she enjoys settling down for cuddles and shows what a sweet, affectionate girl she really is.

She is confident enough that she could happily be an only dog, provided she has plenty of company. She could also live with another friendly dog as long as they would be happy to tolerate a cheeky, bouncy puppy wanting to play and chase them around!

For someone willing to invest the time and training she deserves, Sybil is going to make an incredibly rewarding companion. She is full of character, eager to learn and has all the ingredients to become an amazing dog with the right guidance.

Sybil will need to attend puppy socialisation classes and her adopters will also need to agree to have her spayed at the appropriate age, as outlined in our adoption agreement.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Marty
description:
I would like an active home filled with love and kindness!

I am young enough to be an only be homed with other dogs or even live with cats.

I would love a garden to run and play in!

I would love plenty of adventures once I am fully vaccinated!

I am just a puppy! Please be patient with me while I learn the basics!

I will need to be neutered! It's important!

16-07-26 Marty is a wonderful little puppy who was born at Many Tears after his mum arrived with us pregnant.Martyhas had the best start in our care, enjoying lots of cuddles, playtime with his siblings, and all the love he could ask for.

Like any puppy,Martyis full of fun and curiosity, and absolutely loves playing with toys and his siblings. He is looking for an active, loving home where he can continue to learn, grow, and enjoy all the adventures that puppyhood brings.

As he is still very young,Martycould be the only dog in a home where someone is around for most of the day, or he could happily live with a kind resident dog who doesn't mind sharing their space. He could also live with dog-savvy cats who will respect his boundaries, and sensible, dog-savvy children who understand he is still growing and learning.

Having never experienced life in a home,Martywill need patient and understanding adopters to help him with house training and all the other basics. Once fully vaccinated, he will enjoy learning to walk on a lead and exploring the world around him.Marty's adopters will also need to commit to attending puppy socialisation and training classes to help him grow into a confident, well-rounded adult dog, and agree to have him neutered when he is old enough.

Martyis a lovely little puppy with so much love, fun, and companionship to offer, and he is sure to bring endless joy to his forever family.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Cooper
description:
I would like a calm and quiet, adult only home.

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I have never met cats.

I would love a good sized garden to explore!

I am a beginner to walking on a lead, but would like an active home.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home!

Meet Cooper, a lovely boy who has travelled all the way from Romania to begin his new life and search for his forever home. He is a sweet boy who was quite overwhelmed when he first arrived, but is starting to come out of his shell more and more each day. He can be worried when he first meets new people, but will now greet his kennel mummy with a wagging tail every day! Despite his worries, he is easy to handle and in the right home we think he will truly thrive.

Cooper absolutely adores other dogs and takes great comfort from having canine friends around him, so he will need another kind, confident and playful resident dog in his new home to be his companion and show him the ropes. He is also beginning to discover the joy of toys and is enjoying learning how to play, which is so sweet.

As Cooper has never lived in a home before, his adopters will need to be patient, kind and understanding while he settles in and learns all about home life, including new skills like house training. He is very much a beginner when it comes to walking on a lead and harness, but he is already starting to enjoy his walks and would love an active home where he can continue exploring the world at his own pace.

Cooper would love to find adopters who are experienced with dogs and, whilst not essential, experience with Romanian rescue dogs would be a bonus. He would be happiest in a calm, quiet, adult only home where he can settle in at his own pace and continue building his confidence. With patience, understanding and plenty of love, Cooper will start to discover just how wonderful his life can now be. In return, this gentle boy will reward his family with the privilege of watching him blossom into the happy and loved companion he was always meant to be.

He has tested negative prior to arrival for heartworm, lyme disease, ehrlichia, anaplasma, giardia, leishmania and brucellosis.

When Cooper first arrived, his world was filled with fear. He would quietly tuck himself away at the back of his kennel, very unsure of all his surroundings and who we all were. It was so heartbreaking to see how scared cooper really was.

But every day, with kindness, patience and love, Cooper has taken another tiny step forward. Those tiny steps have turned into giant leaps. Today, the Cooper who once hid away greets his kennel mum with a wagging tail every morning and spends his days happily playing or relaxing with his kennel friends. His personality is really staring to come through and he is slowly learning that the world isn't such a scary place.

When Cooper first arrived, he'd had very little experience walking on a lead. Now he is improving so much more, he even happily takes trips down to our big play yard!

Cooper still finds unfamiliar places and new people a little scary, but it doesn't take long for him to realise he's safe. Once he trusts you, you see the sweetest, gentlest soul who simply wants to love and be loved.

Cooper has done everything we've asked of him. He's been brave. He's learned to trust. He's opened his heart. Now, we're asking for someone to do the same for him.

This beautiful young boy shouldn't be growing up behind kennel doors. He should be learning about the world from the comfort of a sofa, chasing toys around a garden, falling asleep after long walks, and knowing what it feels like to be in a home.

I am so incredibly proud of the progress Cooper has made and I have no doubts in a home with all the patience and kindness he will blossom into the most amazing loving family member anyone could wish for.

Meet Cooper, a lovely boy who has travelled all the way from Romania to begin his new life and search for his forever home. He is a sweet boy who is quite overwhelmed to find himself here, but is starting to settle in and adjust to his new surroundings. Understandably, he is feeling quite scared at the moment, especially when he first meets new people. With those he knows, he is starting to come out of his shell and its lovely to see his confidence growing. Despite his worries, he is easy to handle and in the right home we think he will truly thrive.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Daisy
description:
I’d like a home filled with love and kindness!

I need a larger, male, doggy friend in my new home. I haven't met cats!

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

Please be patient with me, I need to learn house training and lead walking.

I had an entropion on both lower eyelids, but these have now been surgically repaired

02-06-26 Daisy is a scared Cocker Spaniel who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start.She is finding human interaction very difficult at the moment and will often move away if we get too close. Daisy is worried by handling, so she will need careful and gentle management while she learns that people can be kind. Having had very little life experience, much of the world is still new and overwhelming to her, and she will need patient adopters who are willing to let her progress at her own pace.

At the moment, Daisy is feeling a little less brave and is finding comfort in the company of her kennel friends. We feel it would be best for her to be re-homed with another large, male dog for support and companionship. Having a confident canine friend by her side will help her feel more secure as she settles into home life.

As Daisy has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. Her adopters will need to be understanding and prepared to guide her through these new experiences with patience and reassurance.

Daisy prefers to enjoy her meals on her own, so mealtimes are carefully managed here and should continue to be managed in the same way in her new home. Feeding her separately helps prevent any confusion over whose food belongs to whom and allows her to feel more relaxed. Treats should also be given thoughtfully and managed in the same way to ensure a positive experience for everyone.

Daisy could share her home with quiet, older dog-savvy teenagers aged 16 and over who can appreciate and understand that she will need a gentle, quiet introduction to the home and her own space to settle in.

Daisy has arrived with bilateral entropion of the lower eyelids, which have now been surgically repaired by our vets. She is also underweight, so we will be helping her reach and maintain a healthier weight during her stay with us. This will need to be continued in her new home to ensure she remains happy and healthy.

Although Daisy is finding life quite frightening right now, there is a lovely girl underneath those worries. With time, patience, and the support of a canine companion, we are confident that she will begin to blossom and discover that the world is not such a scary place after all.

Daisy has been given the Gift of Life by Lynn Carrivick

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Fox
description:
I could live with dog-savvy teenagers who respect my space.

I need a doggy friend in my new home. I haven’t met cats so I’m unsure.

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

Please be patient with me, I need to learn house training and lead walking.

I have been spayed & am ready for my forever home!

Fox is a nervous lady who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. Since arriving, Fox has really started to blossom and is showing us more of her lovely personality every day. She is a wonderfully smiley girl whose confidence continues to grow, and she will now happily approach people for a gentle chin scratch before settling nearby and relaxing in their company.

Fox still takes a great deal of comfort from her canine companions and relies heavily on her kennel friends for reassurance. During her time at the rescue, she has shared her kennel with several different dogs and has been an absolute angel with every one of them, regardless of their size or gender. She is a gentle and social girl who clearly finds confidence from having another dog by her side.

Fox will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she continues to settle in and find her paws. A canine companion will be an important part of helping Fox feel safe and continue building her confidence.

As Fox has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. She will need patient adopters who are happy to take things at her pace and allow her confidence to build gradually.

In the right home, Fox could share her space with dog-savvy teenagers aged 16 and over who can appreciate and understand that she will need a gentle, quiet introduction to the home and her own space to settle in.

Fox is a sensitive and gentle girl who is trying her best to be brave. She has already come such a long way, and with continued patience, support, and the companionship of another dog, she will continue to blossom into a happy and confident companion.

Fox has been given the Gift of Life by Judie Fisher

Fox is really blossoming during her time at the rescue. She’s a wonderfully smiley girl whose confidence grows a little more every day. She will now happily approach people for a gentle chin scratch and is content to lie down and relax nearby.Fox still takes a great deal of comfort from her canine companions and relies heavily on her kennel friends for reassurance. During her time at the rescue, she has shared her kennel with several different dogs and has been an absolute angel with every one of them, regardless of their size or gender.

Fox is a nervous lady who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is currently too worried to ask for fuss, but her confidence is slowly beginning to grow. At the moment, she will only approach when she has the reassurance of hiding behind one of her kennel friends, showing just how much she relies on their support.

Fox gets on wonderfully with her kennel friends and relies heavily on their company for comfort, so she will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

Fox is a sensitive and gentle girl who is trying her best to be brave. With time, patience, and the support of another dog, she will begin to feel safe and slowly blossom into a much more confident companion.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Zinc
description:
Zinc is not ready for adoption at this time. Keep checking back for updates.

I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a resident dog to help me feel safe but I haven’t met cats yet so I'm not sure

I’m still shy, so I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I am learning about house training in my foster home!

Please read below for more information about my health!

Zinc is a truly sweet and gentle older lady who came to us from a breeder. Sadly, Zinc arrived to us with very limited vision as she had cataracts in her left eye and no sight in her right eye. After visiting a specialist, Zinc underwent cataract surgery, and having some sight again has made a wonderful difference to her life. While she was always a happy, waggy-tailed little dog even without vision, she is now much more confident and curious about the world around her, happily exploring her surroundings, meeting new people and dogs, and even enjoying short walks along the beach with her foster family and their dog.

Zinc has previously had an abdominal hernia successfully repaired and has had two teeth extracted. She has very few teeth remaining and therefore may benefit from a softer diet depending on her comfort when eating. She also previously received treatment for itchy skin, which has now improved greatly,  her skin is in very good condition and her coat is now soft and shiny.

Zinc is in a loving foster home where she has settled in beautifully. Despite having never lived in a home before arriving with us, Zinc took to home life like a duck to water and has remained clean both day and night with regular trips into the garden.

According to her foster mummy, Zinc absolutely loves exploring and being involved in daily life. She is very food-motivated and will happily supervise any activity in the kitchen in the hope that something tasty might appear. In fact, she loves food and treats, really anything edible! She also enjoys pottering around the garden, keeping an eye on anyone doing the gardening, and will occasionally burst into playful zoomies around the house or garden, acting more like a puppy than a senior dog.

Zinc gets on very well with other dogs and would be happiest living with dog-friendly companions of any size or breed. She enjoys meeting other dogs out and about and is comfortable sharing her space, as long as they are kind and tolerant.

Other than food, Zinc’s absolute favourite thing is human contact. She actively seeks out cuddles and will let you know she wants to be picked up by gently pawing at your legs. She loves joining her people on the sofa in the evenings and happily accepts cuddles from visitors too. Zinc is incredibly easy to handle and truly doesn’t have a bad bone in her body, everyone who meets her falls a little bit in love with her.

Zinc is a gentle, loving soul who is looking for a calm and quiet home where she can enjoy her golden years surrounded by love, comfort, and cuddles. Whilst she is an older dog, she now has a wonderful new lease of life and her tail rarely stops wagging.

Anyone considering adopting Zinc must be prepared to continue her eye drop routine and attend occasional follow-up appointments with an eye specialist to monitor and protect her remaining eye. Please note that this eye would not be covered by insurance.

Zinc will make a devoted and affectionate companion for someone with a big heart and a loving home.

Having sight has made a big difference to Zinc, whilst she was a very happy Waggy tailed little dog without sight, now, she explores everywhere, loves meeting other dogs, and people, even having short walks along the beach, with our own dog (a big GSD).

Zinc would be happiest living with dog friendly dogs, any size or breed.  She loves her food and treats in fact anything edible, her skin is now in very good condition plus she's no longer skinny and her coat is soft and shiny.

She loves to join us on the sofa at night and really enjoys a cuddle, even from visitors, letting us know by pawing at our legs to be lifted up. Zinc is very easy to handle with not a bad bone in her body plus rarely has a toilet accident indoors.Whilst Zinc is an older dog, she acts at times like a puppy, doing zoomies around the garden or indoors, she's also keen to supervise us gardening, or anyone in the kitchen cooking as she loves food.

Anyone considering adopting Zinc must be prepared to carry on her eye drop schedule plus visit an eye specialist for future tests to make sure her one remaining eye is maintained, her eye would not be covered by insurance.Zinc is really enjoying her new lease of life as her tail never stops Wagging..

Little Zinc has settled into living in a home like duck to water. She has been clean both day and night with regular garden trips.

Zinc's eyes will be assessed again in January, she is aware of what's around her, and nothing stops her from getting around, especially if  she can smell food, as she has a very good appetite.

Zinc is still stiff in her back legs, but it doesn't stop her getting around and exploring.

Zinc is great with other dogs, she's needs mates that are tolerant for her bumping into them now and again.

Other than food, Zinc's favourite pastime is seeking out a human to be picked up and cuddled. They don't come much sweeter than this little girl. Everyone who meets her are smitten.

Zinc is a truly sweet and gentle older lady who has come to us from a breeder. She may be a little unsure of the world at first, but once she knows you, she’s all love and wags. If you call her name and she’ll happily waddle over with her tail going a mile a minute, ready for a cuddle or to curl up in your lap. She adores a good snuggle and would love nothing more than to share your bed or snooze beside you.

Zinc has never lived in a home before, so she will need patient adopters who can gently guide her through house training and all the new experiences of family life. She relies heavily on the company of other dogs, so she must have a kind, confident resident dog in her new home to help show her the ropes and give her reassurance.

Zinc is an older girl with a few age-related challenges. She has full cataracts in her left eye and no sight in her right, and has been seen by a specialist who has determined that she may need cataract surgery in one of her eyes if she is deemed as a candidate, and may need the other removed. She is anaemic so nothing can be done for her at this time, but if things do improve, we will consider moving forward with the surgeries. Her adopters will need to be aware of this and will need to be in the position to support her financially as it will not be covered by insurance. Her back legs are slightly twisted and her stifle joints a little weak due to age. She has also had an abdominal hernia successfully repaired, as well as 2 teeth extracted, and is currently receiving treatment for itchy skin. She does not have very many teeth left and may benefit from a soft diet depending on her comfortability levels while eating.

Despite everything, Zinc’s spirit shines through, she’s affectionate, gentle, and loves nothing more than being close to her people. She will make a wonderful, devoted lap dog and a loyal companion for someone with a kind heart and the patience to help her enjoy her golden years in comfort and love.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Gabriel
description:
I need an adult-only experienced home that understands me.

I can be an only dog or live with other dogs. I cannot live with cats.

I may be shy now, but once I have settled I would love a nice garden I can play in!

I love going for walks, and would love an active home!

I know some tricks & walk well on the lead but will need help with housetraining.

I have been neutered and am ready to find my forever home.

Gabriel originally came to us from a breeder, and for him the world felt like a very frightening place. He was worried of people, the new environments, and unfamiliar experiences left him feeling overwhelmed, and even something as simple as having a lead put on was a huge challenge. He needed time, patience, and gentle encouragement to begin to feel safe.

Gabriel was adopted for a short period, but unfortunately it was not the right fit for him, and he was returned after displaying unwanted behaviours in the home and unfortunately making the wrong choices when he felt uncomfortable in a situation, which did result in Gabriel biting. He will need a quiet adult-only home without resident or visiting children to ensure he can get the structure he is needing. Gabriel can struggle with visitors coming to the house so will need a home without too many visitors and the ability to put him in a different room when people do some over. He will need adopters who are willing to meet him multiple times to develop a bond with him!

Gabriel requires a highly structured, boundary-focused home with experienced owners who are confident, calm, and consistent. He is not a dog who should be allowed on the sofa or cuddling up with toys and we would not want him to be set up for failure. Instead, he needs clear rules, his own space, and guidance that helps him feel secure and understand what is expected of him. His adopters will need to understand that he has potential to guard high value items or sleeping spaces and will need to work with him. Respecting his boundaries is essential to his wellbeing and success.

He will need both physical and mental stimulation every single day, whatever the weather. Regular walks, enrichment, and brain games are a must to keep his active mind engaged and to help him remain balanced and settled.

Despite his difficult start, Gabriel has made remarkable progress. These days, he can’t wait for his walks and becomes visibly excited when he sees his lead come out. He now allows his harness to be put on and walks beautifully, thoroughly enjoying his outdoor adventures.

Gabriel is a very clever boy and a true foodie, which makes training both rewarding and enjoyable. He already knows cues such as “sit” and “paw” and loves learning new skills. He thrives with clear guidance, consistency, and ongoing training, and will do best with an experienced owner who enjoys working with dogs and building a strong, structured relationship.

Gabriel forms very strong bonds with his people and is deeply loyal once trust is established. Because of this, he has shown signs of separation anxiety and will need adopters who are home for the majority of the time. He is not suited to being left alone for long periods and will thrive best with someone who can make him a central part of their daily routine. While he is not a dog who seeks constant physical closeness, once a bond is formed he does enjoy affection and gentle fuss in his own way.

He could live as the only dog, enjoying one-to-one attention, or potentially alongside a calm, confident resident dog who respects his space. He will need a home with no cats or small furries.

Gabriel has worked incredibly hard to overcome his fears and has already come so far. He is now searching for a forever home that will offer him understanding, structure, commitment, and the chance to live a fulfilled life. For the right, experienced person, Gabriel will be a loyal and rewarding companion.

Gabrielwill need more than one meet and greet with his prospective adopters to allow him time to build a bond and feel comfortable with them before being adopted.

This week we’ve had a heatwave which has meant alot of our rescue dogs have needed their ears checked incase they’ve had any mucky build up - Gabriel was one of the unlucky few that have needed some TLC in this area!!We’ve recently had a new meds person who has been visiting Gabriel to do his ear cleans & he has been an absolute angel. He’s even been given the nickname Angel GabrielIt’s just a testament to Gabriel that yes he can be unsure of new people, but once he knows you he truly is such a wonderful boy. He just needs someone to take that chance on him, show him the love he’s desperate to have & he’ll return it tenfold.Gabriel has been waiting such a long time now, and seeing a boy so full of love & hope is heartbreaking when we can’t reassure him that his time will come soon!!!

Today, I worked with Gabriel again for the first time in months. I was a little worried he’d have forgotten, however, it’s safe to say the bond previously made still remains.Gabriel is such an excited little boy. He lives for treats. He’s such a clever and obedient boy when the treats come out. Usually, he’s full of energy and excitement. Once the treats are out, he’s still and sits like the perfect little boy we know he can be.As he can be very busy, I’ve always thought it essential to take him out on walks, the longer the better. As mentioned in a previous update, he loves walks! Once you pull the lead out, you get a similar reaction to the treats.The longer the walk the better. Walks are so good for this gorgeous boy. They keep him stimulated and help shave off the pounds he gains from everyone giving him treats!On the whole, I was so happy to see my boy again. So happy, so loyal, keen to go on walks and he listened so well. I’m so proud of the progress he’s made since I last saw him!Gabriel still can be a challenge and, he does not like everyone. Once you’ve made that bond with him though, it stays. Don’t overlook this boy. He’s such a beautiful, intelligent and fun dog!

This boy has my heart, once you form that special bond with him I do believe it's for life. He is very loving and enjoys a cuddle and a fuss from people he feels comfortable around, as his adopter you'll hopefully get that bond through spending time with him and letting him go at his own pace.Gabriel, as most bockers do, lives for his food and treats, whenever I run him in from being in the yard I always leave a small treat in his bed or somewhere in the back for him to find, and he absolutely loves this he gets so excited knowing he'll get a treat and runs straight to the back of his kennel to find it.He loves his walks and his time in the big yard. He's the curious, adventurous type who just wants to investigate everything, a 10 minute stroll up the road can take us an extra 20-25 minutes to stroll back because he likes to explore, but I don't mind, seeing him happy makes me happy I'd never want to stop him from exploring the world.Gabriel would need an experienced, adult only home (view profile for explanation), he would need someone who is active and can provide him with mental and physical stimulation daily. He is a very smart boy and can pick things up quite quickly which is why, with the help from some treats I think he would pick up toilet training rather quickly.Please don't overlook this handsome guy just because he's not your small and fluffy type dog, Gabriel is a special boy with a lot of love to give, and for that right person/people he would become a true companion and best friend.

Gabriel, a stunning Bocker with a huge personality and an even bigger heart. This handsome boy is full of life and enthusiasm, always ready to embrace whatever the day brings. Gabriel thrives on affection and absolutely loves a good fuss and cuddle — he’s a real people-dog who enjoys being close to those he trusts.

Gabriel is a clever lad with so much potential. He’s already showing us just how intelligent he is as he responds to basic commands! With the right guidance and training , we know he’ll flourish and continue to grow in confidence, not that he lacks any confidence! He also walks beautifully on the lead, making him a joy to take out on adventures.

Sadly, Gabriel has been returned to us, and we are now searching for the right forever home where he can truly settle and feel secure. Gabriel would be best suited as an only dog, in a calm and quiet home environment. We are looking for a home with minimal visitors and no children, as busy households can feel overwhelming for him.

Gabriel can display guarding behaviours, so he will need experienced owners who understand this and can manage the home environment appropriately to support him. With the right knowledge, structure, and patience, Gabriel will thrive and continue to build trust.

Gabriel has so much love to give to the right person. With understanding, consistency, and kindness, he will make an incredibly loyal and devoted time

Gabriel is a stunning Bocker who is full of life, love, and character! This gorgeous boy is bursting with energy and adores nothing more than a good fuss and a cwtch.He’s incredibly smart, and we just know it wouldn’t take long for him to be trained to a very high standard – he’s already showing us how bright he is every day. Gabriel also walks beautifully on the lead, making him a joy to take out and about.Now all he needs is a forever family who can give him the love, attention, and adventures he deserves. With his brains, energy, and big heart, Gabriel is sure to make someone the most wonderful companion.

Gabriel has come to us from a breeder and has made incredible progress since arriving with us! When he first came, he was nervous around new people and worried about having a lead put on, which made walks and new kennel staff introductions a real challenge. Now, he gets excited for walk time and happily allows us to put on both his lead and harness with no issues.This handsome boy is a true foodie and loves learning new tricks. He already knows “sit” and “paw,” so with trust and patience, teaching him more tricks should be a fun and rewarding experience. Gabriel needs a confident, experienced, owner who will provide clear boundaries and structure, as he can be cheeky if he thinks he’s in charge as well as an adult-only home where he can continue to learn. Gabriel has the confidence to be an only dog in a home with can allow him the dedication and time he needs, or he can be homed with a resident companion that he can continue to learn and grow from, but will need a home with no cats or small furries.Once Gabriel knows and trusts you, he is affectionate, loyal, and loves nothing more than sharing his goofy charm. Underneath his shy moments, he is a beautiful boy with a heart full of love. He is looking for someone who will continue his training, set clear rules, and enjoy all the affection, loyalty, and joy he has to give.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Topps
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I would like a daily walk and lots of cuddle time.

I'm learning house training and building confidence on the lead.

I have a slight click in my left stifle joint, read below for more info!

Topps arrived with us from a breeder and, at first, was an extremely worried and shut-down little girl. Everything was overwhelming for her, and she found human contact very frightening, as she didn’t yet understand how gentle and loving hands could be. With time, patience, and kindness, Topps has started to blossom and has already made such wonderful progress. From barking at her kennel mummy and wanting little to do with people, she now feels comfortable sitting close, taking treats, giving sweet nose nudges, and even sharing little kisses. She has recently begun to accept gentle ear tickles when she is feeling brave, a huge step for her!

Topps has now moved to a foster home, where she is starting to learn all about home life and new skills like house training for the first time. In the home, she is still a little nervous as she adjusts to her new environment. Despite this, she will spend time in the same room as her carers, happily follow for food or to venture outside, and is comfortable moving throughout the home. Although she approaches her carers with encouragement from her canine friend or the help of a treat, she will avoid close contact at times. However, she is still very waggy-tailed and really gains confidence from her furry friends.

Topps still has a way to go and will need an experienced, adult-only home where she can continue to build her confidence at her own pace. She must have at least one kind and confident resident dog, who will play an important role in showing her the ropes and helping her feel safe. A secure garden is essential for her as she continues to learn about lead walking and house training. Topps will tolerate being handled for check overs or to have her harness put on, but she is wary and needs slow handling and reassurance.

This lovely girl may be worried at first in her new home, but with patience, reassurance, and love, she has every chance of blossoming further into the happy, content little dog she was always meant to be. Topps has already shown us what she is capable of; now she just needs someone to take a chance on her and give her the forever home she so truly deserves.

Our vet has noted a slight click in her left stifle joint on palpation. This is not currently causing her any discomfort and does not require treatment at this time, but it may need monitoring in the future. We recommend being mindful of excessive jumping on and off furniture or other jarring movements. Gentle exercises to help build and maintain muscle, such as controlled walking and stepping up and down kerbs, will be beneficial.

Topps is coming along really well, although she is still quite cautious at times. Despite this, she is such a happy girl and will happily come over when she would like some attention. She absolutely loves going out for walks and gets very excited as soon as the harness comes out. She also really enjoys seeing other dogs while out and about, often going over to greet them, and she has even started to approach people on walks too. Topps is very food-motivated and takes treats from people very gently.

Topps is a happy, tail-wagging girl who has settled in wonderfully with the other dogs in the house. She loves cuddling up and sleeping alongside her canine friends, and she gets along well with other dogs she meets too.

While Topps is confident around dogs, she is still a little cautious and hesitant around people. That said, when she feels ready, she will happily come over for a gentle chin scratch or an ear rub, all on her own terms.

Topps would thrive in a home that is patient and understanding, giving her the time she needs to build trust and feel completely comfortable. With her cheerful nature and love for canine company, she is sure to bring joy and companionship to the right family.

My cheeky little lady Topps has been with me for just over a month now and she’s come out of her shell SO much in that time. When she arrived on my section she really didn’t want anything to do with me and would more or less bark at me constantly. Progress has been slow and steady over the past few weeks, but now she’s very comfortable being around me, she’ll sit near me, she’ll take treats from me, she’ll give me little kisses and nudges with her nose, AND she’s just started to accept a little tickle under her ears when she’s feeling brave … which is huge!!! I’m super happy with how well she’s doing and it gives me great hope for her future. If she can progress this much in a kennel environment, just imagine what she could do in a home with her own humans!?Topps will still need a resident dog in her new home and still no children as I do think that would be too overwhelming for her. She may be quite worried when she first gets home, but with lots of reassurance and patience, I do think she will blossom in time. She really just needs somebody to take a chance on her now, and show her how beautiful life can actually be. She deserves the world and more, so please don’t keep her waiting much longer.

Topps has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. She is an extremely worried and shut down girl at the moment who is finding everything very overwhelming. She is very frightened of being handled, as she doesn’t yet understand that human hands can be gentle and loving.

Topps is looking for a calm and quiet, adult only home with experienced adopters who understand the needs of scared ex-breeding dogs. She will need lots of patience, time and understanding to help her slowly come out of her shell.

She must have at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home who can be her friend and take her under their wing. This will play a vital role in helping Topps build confidence and settle in to life in a loving home.

Topps has never lived in a home before, and she will likely find this new environment very overwhelming at first. She will need gentle support and encouragement as she begins to learn about house training, and her adopters will need to take things at her pace.

She does not yet walk on a lead and will need to start slowly, practicing in a secure garden once she is ready. Because of how fearful she currently is, a secure garden is essential for her safety and confidence building.

Topps has a long journey ahead of her, but with the right family, she has the potential to transform into a happy and content dog. If you have the time, love, and experience to help her on this journey, please consider offering Topps the second chance she so desperately deserves.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Sally
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a larger, resident male dog to help me settle in and I have met cats fine.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I am learning to walk on a lead and harness, but it's still very new for me.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

Sweet little Sally has come to us from a breeder to begin her search for a loving forever home. When she first arrived, she was understandably feeling a little worried to find herself here, but she has been trying so hard to be brave and has really started to come out of her shell.

Sally is growing into the funniest and cutest little dog, and for such a small girl she certainly has a big personality that is beginning to shine through. Her confidence continues to grow, and she now happily enjoys receiving fuss and attention from people she trusts. Sally has even started her harness training and is although it's still very new to her, she is enjoying going on walks and exploring.

Sally is a super little dog who is just waiting for someone to give her the time, patience, and love she needs to continue building her confidence. With the right home and lots of kindness, we are sure she will blossom into a wonderful companion.

She will be looking for a calm, kind and understanding home with adopters who are around most of the time and who will allow her to settle in at her own pace. She has never lived in a home before, so everything in a home environment will be new to Sally, so she will need gentle guidance as she continues learning about everyday life and learns new skills such as house training.

Sally absolutely loves other dogs and gains a lot of comfort from having canine friends around her. She will need another dog in her new home to help her feel confident, but she would prefer the company of a smaller male dog. Sally can be a little bossy with female dogs, so she is best suited to living with a male companion. She has met a cat at the rescue fine (please see the video in her gallery!) and so maybe possibly be able to be homed with a dog savvy cat.

This sweet girl just needs someone to give her the chance she deserves, and we are sure she will repay your love tenfold.

Sally has a lick granuloma on her left front leg, which is healing, and an old scar on her left hip. She has had an umbilical hernia repair at the time of her spay and is being treated for bilateral conjunctivitis.

Sally has been given the Gift of Life by Carolina Olsen

Super little Sally is doing so well. I absolutely adore this lovely lady. She grows in confidence every day, and it really hasn’t taken her all that long to come out of her shell.

Sally still needs a little time with new people to feel safe, but she tries so hard, and once she feels comfortable with you, her funny personality shines through, from showing you her full-body rolls, to running over for gentle fusses, or scrambling all over you for foodie treats. I’m very proud of how hard she tries each day to be brave.

Today, Sally enjoyed another little walk around the rescue with her new kennel companion. Sally is by no means confident on the lead yet and will need gentle encouragement and patience to continue her learning journey and start to relax on her walks.

Sally looks to her kennel buddies for support, and her perfect home will already have a friendly (male) resident dog to help show her the way. Sally is best suited to a quiet, adult-only home, ideally with knowledge and/or experience of ex-breeding dogs.

Sally is just the growing into the funniest, cutest of little dogs. For a small being she has a big personality and it’s really starting to shine through. She grows ever confident, and happily takes fusses from me and today successfully begun harness training.

Sally is a super little dog just waiting for a loving home to help continue bringing her out of her shell. Sally will need a friend in her new home but prefers the company of smaller male dogs, she can be a bit bossy with female dogs and is best suited to an adult only home.

Sweet little Sally has come to us from a breeder to begin her search for a loving forever home. She is a very gentle girl who is understandably feeling a little worried to find herself here, but she is trying so hard to be brave.

Sally will gently approach you for a little sniff and is already showing what a sweet nature she has, she just needs time, patience and reassurance to help her confidence grow. With the right home and lots of kindness, we are sure she will blossom into a lovely companion.

She will be looking for a calm, kind and understanding home with adopters who are around most of the time and who will allow her to settle in at her own pace. Everything in a home environment will be completely new to Sally, so she will need gentle guidance as she learns about everyday life and new skills such as house training and how to walk on a lead.

Sally absolutely loves other dogs and gains a lot of comfort from them, so she will need at least one other kind and gentle resident dog in her new home to be her friend and show her the way. Sally will need a larger, male dog in her home and cannot live with females or smaller dogs.

This sweet girl just needs someone to give her the chance she deserves and she will repay your love tenfold.

Sally has a lick granuloma on her left front leg, which is healing and an old scar on her left hip. She has had an umbilical hernia repair at the time of her spay and is being treated for bilateral conjunctivitis.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Heidi
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I haven't tried walks just yet and will find this a bit overwhelming at the moment.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I am spayed and ready to find my forever home.

Heidi has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for a very special forever home where she can begin the next chapter of her life. When she arrived at the rescue, she was a very scared little girl who was feeling quite overwhelmed. She is clearly not used to being handled and finds human interaction very worrying. If you move slowly and gently, she will accept being picked up and handled, she simply needs time to learn that people can be kind.

Heidi has recently moved to a wonderful foster home, where she has started to experience home life for the first time ever. She spent her first few days feeling very unsure and choosing to hide in her safe space but has since started to explore the home more and now will even choose to sit at her fosterers feet when she's working on lay on the bed next to the sofa when she's on the sofa, showing that she is already making encouraging progress.

Heidi will be looking for a calm and quiet, adult only home with patient adopters who understand the needs of scared ex-breeding dogs. She will need plenty of time, reassurance and TLC as she gradually settles in, builds trust and starts to discover the world around her. Progress may be slow, but watching her confidence grow will be incredibly rewarding.

She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs and will need at least one other kind, confident resident dog in her new home. Having a canine friend by her side will help her feel secure and show her that life in a loving home isn't so scary after all.

As Heidi had never lived in a home before moving to foster, her adopters will need to be kind and understanding as she learns all about home life and new skills like house training. She will also need to learn how to walk on a lead and harness, beginning in the safety of the home and garden before gradually building up the confidence to explore further afield.

Heidi is looking for a home that will accept her exactly as she is and give her all the time she needs to blossom. In the right home and with lots of love and TLC, she will thrive.

Heidi has been given the Gift of Life by Hannah Wadsworth

Heidi arrived in foster feeling very unsure and didn't willingly leave her safe space for the first few days. However, she has since started to explore more, although she does like a clear entry.

Heidi will allow a stroke when in her safe place but isn't yet comfortable with it. That said, she now chooses to sit under the table at my feet when I'm working or on the nearby raised bed when I'm on the sofa.

She will take a treat from me when in her safe space, elsewhere it has to be dropped for her to retrieve it. Heidi will need adopters who are willing to work at her pace to help her realise that it is safe to trust.

Heidi has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for a very special forever home where she can begin the next chapter of her life. At the moment, Heidi is a very scared little girl who is feeling quite overwhelmed to find herself here. She is clearly not used to being handled and finds human interaction very worrying. If you move slowly and gently, she will accept being picked up and handled, she simply needs time to learn that people can be kind.

Heidi will be looking for a calm and quiet home with patient adopters who understand the needs of scared ex-breeding dogs. She will need plenty of time, reassurance and TLC as she gradually settles in, builds trust and starts to discover the world around her. Progress may be slow, but watching her confidence grow will be incredibly rewarding.

As Heidi has never lived in a home before, her adopters will need to be understanding as she learns all about home life and new skills like house training. She will also need to learn how to walk on a lead and harness, beginning in the safety of the home and garden before gradually building up the confidence to explore further afield.

Heidi is looking for a home that will accept her exactly as she is and give her all the time she needs to blossom. With patience, understanding and the support of a canine companion, we are sure this sweet girl will slowly learn to trust and enjoy the life she deserves.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Coco
description:
I could live with dog-savvy teenagers who respect my space.

I would like a kind resident dog in my new home. I am fine with cats.

I'd like a secure garden to potter around in.

I can walk on a lead and harness and love to explore quiet areas.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I have had 2 teeth extracted at the time of my spay.

Coco is a wonderful girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. When she first arrived, she was quite shy and overwhelmed, but it didn't take long for her to come out of her shell and start to blossom! She is now a very friendly little girl who loves people and really enjoys having a gentle fuss, though she can still be skittish at times, but with love and TLC her confidence will continue to grow.

She has now moved to a wonderful foster home, where she is experiencing home life for the very first time. She is already settling in well and especially loves her food and treats!

Coco will be looking for a loving home where she can settle in at her own pace and where her adopters have plenty of time to spend with her. She has met children in her foster home and so could be homed with gentle, dog savvy teenagers. She loves other dogs and does rely on them for comfort, so will be looking for a home with another calm and friendly resident dog to be her friend. She is in foster with a cat and is fine with them so may be able to live with a dog savvy cat.

As Coco had never lived in a home before moving to foster, her adopters will need to be kind and understanding as she continues to settle in and learn all about home life. She is doing very well with her house training in foster, but may need some reminders in her new home. She has learned all about walking on a lead and harness and loves to explore quiet areas, so we think she will really enjoy going for walks with her new family.

When Coco arrived with us, we noticed she had a slightly unusual gait. To ensure there were no underlying issues, she underwent x-rays, which thankfully showed no abnormalities.
