She gets around very well and this doesn't seem to effect her, but she can't jump onto furniture so would benefit from having some doggy steps in her home to help her get on and off the sofa etc. She can also lose her balance if playful dogs bump into her, so for this reason she would prefer a home with a calm resident dog who wouldn't knock into her.

Coco is a sweet and affectionate little girl who has so much love to give. This girly pop will bring so much happiness and love into someone’s home. With time, patience, and the support of another dog, she will continue to blossom into a loving and loyal companion.

Coco has had 2 teeth extracted at the time of her spay.

Coco has settled in really well in foster and is enjoying her home comforts. She clearly loves people and approaches for hand licks and little strokes but can be a little skittish if you go to stroke her body. If approached slowly she will allow it and also allow you to pick her up where she snuggles in.

She loves food and will take treats and food gently. She has been completely clean day and night and asks to go out or uses the dog flap.

Coco isn’t interested in toys and prefers to spend her day either sleeping or having a little wander around the garden. She loves going for a walk in a quiet area away from the cars and will get scared if there’s lots of noise but deals with it really well and settles again quickly.

Coco's back legs don’t allow her to jump onto furniture so I feel adopters will need to have little steps for her to climb up easily. Although her legs don’t affect her generally and she walks around absolutely fine and can run around, she does fall over quite easily if another dog bumps into her. For this reason a calmer, older dog who also likes to potter around will be better for her.

Coco currently sleeps in the kitchen with my dogs at night and although for the first night she found it hard to settle, she now happily goes into the kitchen and doesn’t make a noise. Coco is a dream to foster!

Coco has met both cats and children and was absolutely fine with both.

Coco is the definition of loveable! She wants love and affection and is the friendliest little girl you will ever meet. But she doesn’t know how to receive it sometimes. She loves a head scratch but it takes time for her to allow you to touch her anywhere else without her running away.

Seeing her kennel mums in the morning is so exciting that she likes to hug their legs, her back legs were a concern when she first came in and have been checked, weeks later after lots of walk training and socialising and playing with her friends, you can barely notice that she had any issues with her back legs as she’s constantly playing or jumping up to say hello. Her unusual gait can be obvious, but it doesn't hinder her too much. This girly pop will add so much love to someone’s home

Coco is a wonderful girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is quite shy and overwhelmed at first, but once you spend some time with her she is a friendly girl who will approach us for little kisses. With time, love and TLC we think she will truly blossom.

Coco gets on wonderfully with her kennel friends and relies on their company for comfort, so she will need a kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws.

As Coco has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training and the other basics of home life. With a gentle and consistent approach, she will continue to grow in confidence. She has started to learn all about walking on a lead and harness and with some more practice she will begin to gain confidence.

Coco could share her home with dog teenagers who can appreciate and understand that she will need a calm and gentle introduction to the home, along with her own space to settle in.

When Coco arrived with us, we noticed she had a slightly unusual gait. To ensure there were no underlying issues, she underwent X-rays, which thankfully showed no abnormalities.

Coco is a sweet and affectionate little girl who clearly enjoys human company. With time, patience, and the support of another dog, she will continue to blossom and become a loving and loyal companion.

10-06-26 Coco is a wonderful girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is quite shy and overwhelmed at first, but once you spend some time with her she is a friendly girl who will approach us for little kisses. With time, love and TLC we think she will truly blossom.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Toast
description:
I would like a quiet, adult-only home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a resident dog to help me feel safe. I live with a cat in my foster home.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in (6ft+)

I'm harness training currently but need to build my confidence around lead walking.

I will need to learn about house training and lead walking

Toast is a delicate little girl who came to us from a breeder and has been slowly finding her confidence in the world. When she first arrived, she was very nervous and overwhelmed by everything around her, often running to hide in her bed.

Since arriving in foster, it has become clear that Toast is an extremely nervous girl and remains very unsure of new situations. She does not enjoy handling and can panic when she feels pressured, so she will need careful, gentle management and time to feel safe.

Toast is currently living with a cat in her foster home and can live with a dog-savvy cat who respects her space.

She will need a quiet, adult-only home with experienced adopters who understand nervous ex-breeding dogs and can support her without expectation or pressure. A calm, predictable environment will be essential for her ongoing confidence building.

She has come on in some areas, but handling still needs to be approached very carefully. While she has previously shown some progress with being picked up and having a harness fitted, this must always be done with patience and awareness of her limits, as she can become overwhelmed.

Toast is currently working on her harness training, and does allow her foster mummy to put one on her now, but she is not comfortable on a lead just yet and will need continued support and encouragement to build confidence. She is still learning about house training, and seems to be clean in the home when her foster mummy is around but can have a few accidents when she is left alone. With time and patience, she will begin to pick this up and understand the rules of house training.

She need a kind, confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and provide reassurance as she adjusts to home life.

Toast arrived with a low-grade luxating patella on her left side, which may improve with gentle exercise. She has also been treated for mucky ears, right-sided otitis, hot spots, and dermatitis, all of which are improving with care. Her general health will need to continue to be monitored in her new home, with any future treatment provided as needed.

Behind her shy beginnings is a sensitive little dog who is still learning to trust the world around her. With patience, understanding, and the right experienced home, Toast will continue to take small steps forward at her own pace.

Toast has arrived in Foster and continues to be a very frightened little girl and can panic easily if anyone tries to pick her up, so she really doesn’t cope well with handling at this stage. Once she is settled in her bed, she is a little more comfortable and will allow gentle strokes and will accept quiet reassurance and talking.

She is extremely nervous overall and will need a quiet, adult-only home with experienced adopters who understand how to support a dog like her at her own pace, without pressure. She is also currently living with a cat in her foster home.

I can't believe toast has been here so long that she was due another haircut with me!She has come on tremendously for handling and as long as you're gentle she is content with being picked up, as well as having a harness put on. She is on the journey of learning to walk on a lead and it shouldn't be long before she achieves it.

I've worked with Toast for a while now and I have been able to see how much she's grown over time, especially when it comes to being groomed. The bath is still not her favourite place to be, but she tolerates it, and I think she quite enjoys the warm air of the dryer. She ends up with such a cute haircut because she is so well behaved when it comes to the styling.

This small dog has such a big and beautiful personality and I believe in the right home with another confidence dog she will flourish tremendously.

Toast was such a nervous girl when she came in and she has come on really far since then. I had the pleasure of giving her a much needed groom and although she was nervous she took to it like a champ. She will need grooming upkeep in her forever home to look after her evergrowing locks and I think with a few more pampers she will feel so much more comfortable with it.

She's currently having harness training so she can be more comfortable with the sensation and has gotten much better at being picked up and handled although she can still be a bit jumpy at times but once she's in your arms she's quite calm. She still has a ways to go but I'm so proud of her progress so far and can't wait for when she flourishes with her forever family.

Toast is a delicate little girl who has come to us from a breeder to start her journey towards a brighter future. Right now, the world feels like a very big and scary place to her, and her first instinct is to run and hide in her bed. However, once in your arms, she is easy to handle and accepts gentle reassurance with quiet trust.

Toast will need a calm and understanding home with patient adopters who can give her all the time she needs to find her feet. A kind and confident resident dog will be essential to help guide her, show her that the world can be safe, and give her the courage to explore new experiences.

As she has never lived inside a home before, everything will be new to her, from walking on a lead to learning about house training and family life. Gentle encouragement and plenty of love will help her begin to understand how wonderful it feels to be part of a home.

Toast arrived with a low-grade (Grade 1) luxating patella on the left side, which may improve with gentle exercise. She is also being treated for mucky ears and right-sided otitis as well as dermatitis.

Toast may be shy now, but behind those worried eyes is a sweet girl who just needs the right people to help her shine. With kindness, patience, and a little guidance, she will blossom into a loving and loyal companion.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Whimsey
description:
I can live with older, dog savvy teens who are sensible and will allow me to settle.

I could live as an only dog, or with a similar sized canine friend. No cats please!

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I walk well on a lead, but will need guidance in the outside world.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

Whimsey came to us from a breeder as a very worried little girl who found the world quite overwhelming. When she first arrived, she was unsettled and skittish, needing plenty of time and reassurance to help her feel safe. Since then, she has made wonderful progress and has really started to show what a special girl she is.

Whimsey has formed a lovely bond with her carers and now greets those she knows and trusts with enthusiastic cuddles and kisses. It has been a joy to watch her confidence grow, and she is becoming more comfortable with the people around her every day.

While Whimsey has come on tremendously, she can still be nervous and unsure around new people. She will need a calm, patient and understanding home that is willing to give her the time and space she needs to build trust and settle at her own pace. Once she feels safe, her affectionate and loving personality truly shines through. She could also live with older, dog-savvy teenagers aged 16 and over who can respect her need for a gentle introduction and allow her to settle in at her own pace.

Whimsey walks nicely on a lead, but as she can still be sensitive to new experiences, her adopters will need to continue introducing her to the world gently and at a pace she is comfortable with, helping her build confidence without becoming overwhelmed.

Whimsey really enjoys spending time with other dogs and loves socialising with her canine friends in the exercise yards. We now feel she could happily live with another resident dog of a similar size, provided they get along well during a successful meet and greet. Like many dogs, Whimsey can be a little selective about her friendships, so finding the right canine companion will be important. She would also be perfectly happy as the only dog in the home, where she could soak up all the love and attention from her family while continuing to enjoy opportunities to socialise with other dogs on walks. She cannot live with cats.

Whimsey has such a sweet heart and has already shown just how much progress she can make when given kindness, patience and understanding. With the right home behind her, we know she will continue to blossom into a wonderfully affectionate companion and a much-loved member of the family.

Whimsey arrived with an abscess on the right side of her jaw, which has been treated by our veterinary team. Our vets have also noted that she has a fractured canine tooth with no root or nerve exposure. At this stage, no treatment is required, but it should be monitored by her adopters and their own vet. She has also received treatment for a urinary tract infection, which has been successfully managed under veterinary guidance.

Oh, my heart. Whimsey has completely stolen it.

She is honestly one of the most loving, affectionate, and gentle girls I've ever met. The moment I walk into her kennel or sit with her in the yard, she melts into me. All she wants is kisses, cuddles, and to be as close to you as possible. She has so much love to give!

She's doing so well with her dog socialising too and absolutely adores her best friend, Royal.

Whimsey is going to make the most incredible addition to a very lucky family. She deserves so much more than just being someone's dog. She deserves to be a cherished member of the family, included in all of life's little moments, loved unconditionally, and never made to wonder if she's enough again.

I truly can't wait for the day she finds the home she's been waiting for. Whoever gets to call this beautiful girl theirs is going to be incredibly lucky.

Whimsey has come on so well since she first arrived. She’s bonded with me so much and always welcomes me with a big kiss and cuddle.

She can be nervous and unsure around new people, but with time, patience, and understanding, she’s going to make the most wonderful addition to someone’s home.

Although she needs to be the only dog in the home, she can happily socialise with other dogs and loves going down to the big yard with her friends.

Whimsy has come to us from a breeder and is a very worried girl. When she first arrived, she was very unsettled at the moment and could be quite skittish, finding so many of the sights and sounds of the world quite overwhelming. Since arriving she has started to settle in and come out of her shell, but will still need time and TLC as she settles in to her new life. Once she gets to know you, she will quietly approach you and allow a gentle fuss.

Whimsy needs time to learn and to trust, and needs a calm and quiet home home that understands ex-breeding dogs and will allow her the space she needs to learn about trust at her own pace. She will need a calm, patient, and understanding family who are willing to let her take things slowly and gently as she begins to discover the comforts of home life.

She will need a kind and confident resident dog in her home that she can look to and rely on as she learns about home life and settles in. Having a canine companion to guide her will really help build her confidence and show her that the world can be a safe and happy place.

Whimsy does walk well on a lead, but as she can be a bit skittish, her adopters will need to take the outside world at her own pace, allowing her the time she needs to gain confidence in her surroundings without feeling pressured.

Whimsy seems like she has such a sweet heart, and with time, patience, and kindness, she will begin to show her loving and gentle nature. She just needs someone special to help her learn about trust and companionship and to show her that she is safe and loved.

Whimsy has come to us with an abscess on the right side of her jaw, which is being treated by the vets. Our vets have noted that she has one fractured canine, but no root or nerve exposure and so it is advised to leave it at this time, but will need monitoring by her adopters and their vet. She is currently being treated for a UTI and will be rechecked once the course of medication has finished.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Jerry
description:
I need a calm and quiet adult only home with adopters with experience of collies.

I could live with another dog, or by myself. I cannot live with cats.

I would love a garden to run and play in!

I would like an active home and love to go for walks.

I have lived in a home before so know the basics.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home!

Jerry is a lovely boy who originally came to us from a breeder a few years ago and went on to find a wonderful forever home. Sadly, through no fault of his own, he has now returned to our care due to his adopters' ill health.

Jerry is a Collie lover's dream! He is affectionate, clever and eager to be part of the family. He is house trained, walks beautifully on the lead and we are told he has excellent recall too. He is a very smart boy who already knows a range of commands includingsit,wait,off,dropandleave, and he would thrive in an active home where he can be kept mentally and physically stimulated.

Since moving down to one of our field kennels, Jerry has become much more relaxed and settled. Being in a quieter environment has really allowed his wonderful personality to shine. He is incredibly loving and has shown us just how well he can socialise with other dogs. In fact, he has been so calm and sensible around them that he has even been used during one-to-one behaviour sessions with reactive dogs, where he has been an absolute superstar. Because of this, we feel Jerry could happily live with another well-matched dog, although careful introductions would still be important.

Jerry would still be best suited to an adult-only home with adopters who have experience of Collies and rescue dogs. He would love a rural or quieter home where he can continue to enjoy a peaceful lifestyle, and he would benefit from adopters who are around for much of the day and have plenty of time to spend with him.

Jerry has shown some signs of separation anxiety in the past, so his new family will need to continue building up the time he is left gradually. We are told that he is happy being left for short periods but will need patience and understanding while he settles into his new home.

One area where Jerry has made fantastic progress is with his noise sensitivity. While he can still notice unexpected noises, he is coping with them far better than before. Rather than becoming distressed, he is now much more likely to simply look towards his handler as if to ask, "What was that?" before moving on. His growing confidence has been wonderful to see, although his adopters should still be prepared to continue supporting him with gentle reassurance and positive experiences around unfamiliar sounds.

Jerry has settled back into rescue life beautifully. He loves a cuddle, enjoys a good play session and is an incredibly rewarding dog to spend time with. He is a truly special boy with so much love to give, and we know he has all the qualities to make an exceptional companion for the right family.

Jerry previously had a wound on his right paw which caused a limp. This has now healed well and he is no longer showing any signs of lameness.

Jer-bear, Jerry! This handsome boy is going to melt someone’s heart.

Jerry has come in from a home with a variety of noise related triggers but do not let this put you off meeting him, for he is one quick brave border collie. He was once visibly stressed at the sight of a can of pop and more stressed when carbonated drink cans/bottles were opened. He has improved and is not as stressed when he sees a can of pop and due to him moving around the rescue we have given him some time to settle as he wasn’t feeling himself in his previous kennel.

Since moving he has been showing us bit by bit his playful sassy side. He loves going for his morning walk to take a break from the overwhelming kennels, Jerry has even been socialising with other dogs. We have found that he gets on well with other dogs however if put into an overwhelming situation he is likely to redirect onto his kennel buddy so is safer for him and any other dogs if he is not homed with one. We have found that once he is comfortable with his people, he is quick to desensitise to certain triggers.

For example: one of his handlers is diabetic and uses cans of lucozade to treat hypos on walks and in kennels. He has become alert but has not reacted and is less anxious at the sight of cans or bottles now

When I first met Jerry I had heard about his triggers and was very curious, he is a very interesting young dog who is bound to be the light of someone’s life!

I have been working with Jerry for a little while now and have found that his can/bottle related trigger is a bit deeper than we were led to believe, the visual prompt causes him some stress, but found that he is quick to look for comfort. He really enjoys spending time with his doggy friends and looks to humans for comfort, and enjoys going out for walks, he seems to feel more relaxed on walks and have found once triggered by a noise he over analyses the indoor environment not so much the outdoor space.

During Jerry's stay here I plan to slowly introduce new noises and help him to understand that new noises are not necessarily a bad thing.

Jerry is a lovely boy who originally came to us from a breeder a few years ago and went on to find a wonderful forever home. Sadly, through no fault of his own, he has now returned to our care due to his adopters ill health.

Jerry is a Collie lover’s dream! He is affectionate, clever and eager to be part of the family. He is house trained, walks nicely on the lead and we are told that he has good recall too. He is a very smart boy who already knows a range of commands including sit, wait, off, drop and leave, and would thrive in an active home where he can be kept both mentally and physically stimulated.

Jerry would like to be the only dog in his forever home. He would be best suited to an adult only home and would benefit from adopters with collie and rescue dog experience. He would love a rural home, as he can be sensitive to certain sounds and so homes in busier areas wouldn't be suitable for him. He would really benefit from adopters who are around for much of the day and have plenty of time to spend with him.

Jerry does show some signs of separation anxiety, so his new family will need to continue building up the time he is left gradually. We are told that he is happy being left for short periods but will need patience and understanding while he settles. He isn’t keen on car travel, and he can be sensitive to sounds and may react to sounds such as fizzy drink cans or bottles opening, sneezing, coughing, nose blowing, printers, hoovers, microwaves, lorry reversing beeps, motorbike revving, army range noise and fireworks. He will bark towards you but with reassurance he settles quickly. He has never bitten and is just worried but his new owners will need to continue noise desensitization training in the home. It may never be something he entirely overcomes.

Jerry has settled back into the rescue well and is a wonderful boy who is already enjoying snuggles and really loves to play! He is a truly special boy who has so much love to give and is ready to find a calm, understanding home to call his own once again.

He has had a wound on his right paw that has caused a limp, which has since healed and he is no longer showing signs of limping.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Tulip
description:
I'd like a quiet home, but I can live with older, dog-savvy teens!

I need either a male resident dog or a larger dog in my new home.

I'd like a secure garden to potter around in.

I haven't tried walks yet, a secure garden will be fine for now.

Please be patient with me, I need to learn house training and lead walking.

I am spayed and ready to find my forever home.

Tulip is a worried girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start. She is a sensitive little soul who initially found people quite overwhelming and would run away when approached. Since being in our care, Tulip has come on in leaps and bounds. She now happily allows her kennel mummies to pick her up and has grown much more confident with being gently handled. She is also making fantastic progress with her harness training and now calmly lets her harness be put on. While she still takes great comfort from the company of her kennel friends, it has been wonderful to watch her confidence continue to blossom.

Tulip is still a worried girl who can be unsure in new situations, so she will need patient adopters who are willing to let her progress at her own pace without placing any pressure on her. Although she has made incredible progress with us, she will need time to build trust in her new home, and her adopters should be prepared for her confidence to take a little step back while she settles in. She will be best suited to adopters who have experience with scared ex-breeding dogs. She can live with older, dog-savvy teens, aged 16+, who are sensible and will allow her the time she needs to settle at her own pace. A calm, predictable environment will give her the best opportunity to continue building her confidence and feel safe.

Tulip relies on other dogs for comfort, so she will need a kind resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws. She would be best suited to living with either a male dog or a female dog that is larger than her, as she can sometimes struggle with smaller female dogs.

As Tulip has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. However, she has already shown us how capable she is of learning, and her recent progress with handling and harness training gives us every confidence that, with patience and positive guidance, she will continue to flourish.

Tulip may still find the world a little scary at times, but she is a lovely little girl with a gentle nature who is growing in confidence every day. We know that, with time, patience, and the support of a canine companion, Tulip will continue to blossom and discover just how wonderful life can be.

Tulip has come on in leaps and bounds with her handling. She now happily allows her kennel mummies to pick her up and has grown much more confident with being gently handled. She's also making great progress with her harness training and now calmly lets her harness be put on. Tulip is gaining confidence every day, and it's wonderful to see her blossoming.

Tulip is a worried girl who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a new start.

She is a worried little soul who currently finds people quite overwhelming and will run away when approached. Tulip much prefers the company of her kennel friends and takes great comfort from being around them. She is an active and spritely girl with a heart of gold, but at the moment she lacks confidence around humans and is still learning that people can be kind.

Tulip is difficult to handle right now because she is very scared and unsure of our intentions. This is completely understandable given her limited experience with human interaction, and she will need patient adopters who are willing to let her progress at her own pace without placing any pressure on her. She will be best suited to adopters who have experience with scared ex-breeding dogs. She can live with older, dog-savvy teens, aged 16+ that are sensible and will allow her time to settle in at her own pace. A calm, predictable environment will give her the best opportunity to build her confidence and feel safe.

Tulip relies on other dogs for comfort, so she will need a kind resident dog in her new home to help guide her and give her the reassurance she needs as she settles in and finds her paws. She would be best suited to living with either a male dog or a female dog that was larger than her as she can sometimes not get along with smaller female dogs.

As Tulip has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training, lead training, and the other basics of home life. Her adopters will need to be understanding and committed to helping her navigate these new experiences gently and positively.

Tulip may be finding the world a little scary right now, but she is a lovely little girl with a gentle nature. We hope that, with time, patience, and the support of a canine companion, she will continue to gain confidence and discover that life has so many wonderful things to offer.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Reet
description:
I would like a home filled with love and kindness!

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I’d enjoy one or two quiet walks a day and plenty of cuddle time.

I am living in a foster home and doing very well with toilet training

I have been spayed and ready for my new home

Reet has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. When she first arrived, she was a little worried and quite overwhelmed, but it was clear there was a sweet and curious little Beagle waiting to emerge beneath her uncertainty.

During her time at the rescue, Reet gradually began to grow in confidence and show more of her true personality. She proved to be a bright, inquisitive girl with a larger-than-life personality and a real enthusiasm for life. She enjoyed meeting other dogs, exploring new areas, and learning about the world around her. With encouragement (and the promise of a tasty treat!), she even tackled challenges she was initially unsure of. Her confidence on the lead also improved greatly, and she soon began to enjoy her walks and adventures around the rescue.

Reet has now settled beautifully into her foster home, where she has continued to flourish. She is now very comfortable with home life, and the transformation in her has been wonderful to see. Her whole body language has softened, and she is much more relaxed and confident in herself. She is happy to try new things that would have seemed far too daunting just a few weeks ago, even stretching out and relaxing on the sofa!

Scurrying past people is now a thing of the past. Reet happily approaches strangers and will gladly accept a treat from just about anyone she meets. She remains a busy, active little Beagle who loves investigating everything around her, and her love of food certainly hasn't changed! She has settled brilliantly into a home environment, her toilet training is going very well, and she enjoys getting out on slow, sniffy walks where she can take in all the wonderful scents. She also has excellent recall, which is a lovely bonus as she continues to build confidence.

Reet will be looking for a calm and understanding home with patient adopters who are prepared to continue supporting her as she discovers just how wonderful life can be. She will need another kind and confident resident dog in her new home to reassure her and continue showing her the ropes.

Helping a dog like Reet is what makes fostering ( and adopting!) so rewarding. She has already come such a long way and has embraced home life with enthusiasm, curiosity and growing confidence. If you're looking for a sweet, affectionate Beagle with a gentle nature and a love of life's simple pleasures, Reet could be the perfect addition to your family.

Reet has been given the Gift of Life by Patti Tucker

Reet is now very comfortable with home life. Her whole body language has changed; she is much softer and more relaxed. She is happy to try new things, which just a few weeks ago would have been too daunting (like spreading out on the sofa!).

Scurrying past people is a thing of the past. She is happy to approach strangers and will take a treat from absolutely anyone.

Reet loves slow, sniffy walks, and her recall is excellent!

Reet had a busy weekend! We went to our local beagle meet in an enclosed field. There were 15 beagles and their humans too, and Reet made me so proud!

She came up and said hello to everyone, took treats and even had some cuddles from humans. She explored the field, but kept an eye on me and came over to check in every now and then. Such a brave girl.

Then back home and look who's applying for a bbq supervisor position! My husband is delighted that Reet doesn't seem to have that fear of men that is so typical of many ex-breeders. I'm sure it gets her extra treats from him, clever girl!

She is getting more confident at home too and now lets me stroke her. She still has a way to go, but it's very early days, and I'm sure she will become a happy cheeky beagle very soon.

Reet is settling well into her foster home. She is a busy, inquisitive little dog who absolutely adores her food and will take treats from anyone! Toilet training is going very well, and she has been joining us for walks outside too!

In fact, the only stumbling block for Reet is human touch. It still scares her, and she will move away from our hands as quickly as possible. She has only been with us for a week though and I let her settle in with no pressure.

Next week we have some bonding activities planned, extra walks if the weather allows, some touch games - I will keep you posted on Reet's progress!

Helping a dog like Reet is what makes fostering - and adopting! - so rewarding. So if you like beagles, and have the patience, understanding, and commitment to help a sensitive dog learn how gentle human hands can be, then please apply for this wonderful sweet girl!

Reet has been my project dog for the past couple of weeks, during this time my main objective was to encourage this happy curious girl to blossom. She's still got a little way to go, but I'm so proud for how far she's come.

When I first started working with Reet her sweet, curious nature was already there, that's just who she is. Since then she's been meeting new dogs and people and building up the courage to explore new areas of her life at the rescue.

As you can see in the videos she's got a larger than life personality that seems to brighten my day. She's now walking perfectly on the lead with confidence in her stride. She's expressing herself openly around people and dogs in the yard. I also got her to build the courage to climb an obstacle she was wary of, with the help of food of course, she is a beagle after all.

Reet has continued her harness training today. She took some encouragement around a large group of people, but bravely continued despite her fears.She absolutely loves the freedom, and space of the big yard with her friends. And she certainty won't say no to a treat.

Reet has made wonderful progress this week. She is doing really well with her harness training and even ventured down to the big play yard for the very first time today, which she absolutely loved. She gets along great with the other dogs and happily shares her food, beds and toys. Her tail never seems to stop wagging!

Reet is a happy girl who has come to us from a breeder and would love to find an active home! She was a little wary at first, but she is really coming out of her shell as she continues to settle in and is showing herself to be a very curious girl. That being said, she is initially nervous when we approach so you do have to go slow with her and allow her time to warm up to you.Reet will be looking for a calm and quiet home, where there is another kind and confident resident dog in the home that she can look to as she continues to build her confidence. As she has never lived in a home before, she will need patient and understanding adopters while she learns about house training and walking on a lead.Reet isn't yet used to being handled and can be a little worried about this, but she is getting more used to this every day and with time and TLC, we think she will truly blossom.Once she has mastered her lead training, Reek would love plenty of adventures and will make a wonderful companion after she has had time to settle in and adjust to home life.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Cassy
description:
I need a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I don't mind cats!

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I love going on walks, and would love plenty in my new home!

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

Please read below for more information about my health!

Cassy is a sweet girl who has come to us from a breeder and is now ready to start her next chapter. She can be a little overwhelmed by new situations and may be unsure initially, but with time, love and patience she is already showing just how much progress she can make.

Cassy has now moved into a foster home where she is settling in well and learning all about home life for the first time ever. She is trying so hard to be brave, but doesn't actively seek attention yet. If she's on the sofa or in bed, she is happy to be fussed.

Cassy relies heavily on other dogs for comfort and reassurance, so she will need at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help guide her and provide support as she continues to learn about life in a home environment. She has met cats and was very good with them, so could potentially live alongside a feline friend, with sensible introductions.

She had never lived in a home before moving to foster and is already doing well with learning new skills like house training, but does need more practice. Her adopters will need to be patient and understanding as she continues to build confidence and learn about the world around her.

Cassy gets so excited when it's time to go on her walks, and would love to continue this in her new home!

This lovely girl is simply looking for someone willing to take a chance on her, offering the love, patience and reassurance she needs to truly thrive. In return, you'll have the privilege of watching her blossom into the happy, confident dog she is meant to be.

When Cassy arrived with us, it was noted that she had a cherry eye, which was repaired. This has recently popped back out, but at this moment is too small to repair again and so this is something her adopters and their vet will need to monitor. She has also been treated for conjunctivitis. She has had a dental with one tooth extracted, and our vets have noted that one other tooth has a slightly exposed root. This does not require removal at present but should be monitored.

Cassy has been given the Gift of Life by Dee Wickson-Dix

After returning to the rescue to have her cherry eye assessed, Cassy has now moved to foster once again. She is making good progress but is still very nervous. She doesn't actively seek attention yet, but she is trying so hard to be brave. If she's on the sofa or in bed, she is happy to be fussed.

She had her first walk on the lead in garden this morning, with the help of lots of primula cheese!! She will now move past me in a relaxed manner and enjoys cuddles with the other dogs.

She likes to be in the same room as me. She eats well and is food orientated and happily takes a treat from my hand.

She will need  an experienced, quiet home where she can progress at her own pace and be fully supported.

Her cherry eye has not caused any problems, and though it has recurred the vet felt like it did no require a second repair at this time, though will need to be monitored. I wipe her eyes daily.

Cassy recently arrived here in foster and is settling in well. Unfortunately her cherry eye has recurred so she will need to head back to the rescue centre to have this assessed, but she has really enjoyed her week in foster.

She is a lovely girl who is very food motivated and will happily approach with a waggy tail when treats are on offer. If food is around, she’s always hopeful for a little taste! She is very hand shy and can be skittish when approached, but with gentle reassurance and patience she will allow strokes and appears to enjoy them, even though she doesn’t actively seek fuss just yet. Sudden movements can startle her, so she benefits from a calm, slow approach. She likes to quietly follow me around the house but will move away if approached too directly.

She enjoys playing with toys and has a funny habit of carrying a shoe around in her mouth. Like many dogs, she may chew items left on the floor, although she doesn’t go looking for things to chew.

Cassy would suit a calm and quiet household where she can be given the time and space she needs to relax and settle in. She really wants to be brave, and with patience and kindness her confidence should continue to grow. She enjoys the company of other dogs and loves snuggling up with them on the sofa.

She currently sleeps in the kitchen with my dogs at night. She is house trained, and confidently uses the dog flap to go outside to toilet.

Cassy is a sweet girl who has come to us from a breeder and is still building her confidence. She can be a little unsure at first and finds reassurance in the company of other dogs.

She will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home who can help guide her and give her the confidence she needs as she settles in.

Cassy has never lived in a home before, so her adopters will need to be patient and understanding while she learns all about house training and how to walk on a lead. Starting with gentle garden exploration and quiet outdoor time will help her gradually get used to the world around her.

This lovely girl is simply looking for someone willing to take a chance on her, offering the love, patience, and reassurance she needs to truly thrive.

Cassy arrived with cherry eye in her right eye that has since been surgically repaired at the time of her spay. She is also being treated for conjunctivitis. She has had a dental with one tooth extracted and our vets have noted that one other tooth has the root slightly exposed, but have advised that it is not removed at the moment and monitored going forward.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Tippins
description:
I can live with older children, aged 16+, who understand I need time to settle in.

I need a larger doggy friend in my new home. I haven’t met cats yet!

I would like a large, and secure garden that I can settle into.

I do walk on a lead, but am still gaining my confidence and will need support.

Please be patient with me, I haven’t lived in a home before.

I have been spayed and am ready for my forever home!

06-07-26 Tippins is a lovely lady who has come into our care from a breeder. She is a little apprehensive when she first meets new people, but she is already showing signs that her confidence is growing. Once she knows you, she enjoys a fuss and is happy to be handled. Tippins has a big personality and is a fun girl to spend time with.

At the moment, Tippins is feeling a little less brave and is finding comfort in the company of her kennel friends. We feel it would be best for her to be rehomed with at least one other confident resident dog for support and companionship. She has always lived with bigger dogs and seems happiest around them. Tippins can be a little bossy with less confident dogs, so she wouldn't be suitable to live with smaller or younger dogs. We will continue to assess her confidence while she is with us to see if she could adapt to being an only dog. She will need adopters with ex-breeding experience that can understand her needs.

As Tippins has never lived in a home before, she will need to learn all about house training and the other basics of home life. Her new family will need to be patient while she settles in and learns what home life is all about. She can live with older, dog-savvy teens, aged 16+ that can allow her time to settle into a calm environment.

Tippins already walks on a lead, but doesn't seem to be overly confident on this so she will need continued support as she learns, and once she has settled into her new home, she would enjoy one or two walks around the local area with her resident dog so that she can continue lead training.

Tippins is a lovely girl with lots of character. She's already showing us what a sweet dog she is, and we hope that, with time and patience, she'll continue to grow in confidence and enjoy everything home life has to offer.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Berry
description:
I need a quiet, adult only home that understands ex-breeders.

I need to be the only dog in the home, and cannot live with cats!

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I walk well on a lead but I can be frightened, so I will need to take it slow.

I have lived in a home for a short period and may already know the bascis.

I have been spayed and am ready for my forever home!

02-07-26 Berry originally came to us from a breeder in 2022 and was adopted into a home, but she has unfortunately been returned as she was still struggling to live with the resident dog. While she socialises well with other dogs when out and about, she is looking for a home where she can be the only dog, as she has shown resource guarding behaviours around high-value items such as food and treats within the home.

When Berry arrived back here, she was a very worried little girl who preferred to keep her distance. Since then, Berry has made some lovely progress. Although she can still be a little nervous in new situations and with unfamiliar people, she has grown so much in confidence with the people she knows. She now actively seeks out fuss from her handler and, if they stop, she'll gently paw at them and even have a little cry to ask for more attention.

In her previous home, Berry displayed resource guarding behaviours around both dogs and people. Around people, this can be managed by giving her a little space and swapping the item for something of higher value, rather than trying to take it away. Her guarding comes from insecurity and likely reflects a life where she never truly had anything of her own. Because of this, Berry is looking for an adult-only home with adopters who understand resource guarding, have ex-breeder experience and are committed to using kind, positive management techniques.

We have been told that Berry is clean in the home, although she may need a few reminders while settling into a new routine. She walks nicely on the lead and doesn't pull, although she can be timid and easily startled, so she will benefit from gentle encouragement as she continues to gain confidence in the world around her.

Despite her worries, Berry has so much love to give. She is simply looking for a calm, patient home where she can finally feel safe, build trusting relationships, and enjoy life at her own pace. With understanding, consistency, and plenty of kindness, Berry will blossom into a wonderfully loyal and loving companion.

name: Kelly
description:
I would like a calm and quiet home where I can settle in.

I need a resident dog to help me settle in and I have never met cats.

I will need a secure garden while I get used to my new home.

I can walk on a lead and harness and love to explore quiet areas.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I have a very overshot jaw, and I have been spayed.

Kelly is a lovely girl who has come to us from a breeder to find her forever home. When she first arrived, she was quite worried but she soon started to settle in and come out of her shell. She has since moved to a wonderful foster home where she is settling in well and experiencing home life for the first time.

She will be looking for a calm and quiet home, where she can settle in at her own pace. She would love her adopters to be around most of the time and we would consider homing her with gentle, dog savvy teenagers.

Kelly has taken to home life well andreally enjoys pottering around the garden and laying in the shade. She is also happy to take treats nicely and loves her food too! She is happy to accept a fuss, has a waggy little tail and is learning all about playing with toys too. She gets very excited to see her fosterers when they come downstairs in the morning, which is lovely.

Kelly loves other dogs and will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home to be her friend and help her settle in to her new life.

She had never lived in a home before moving to foster, so her adopters will need to be kind and understanding as she continues to learn all about home life. She is already doing well learning about house training. She has already learned how to walk on a lead and harness and we think she will really enjoy going for walks in quiet areas as she builds up her confidence.

She is a truly wonderful girl and we think that whoever adopts her is going to adore her!

She has arrived with an overshot jaw that does not bother her, but should be monitored in her new home to ensure she is comfortable.

Kelly has been given the Gift of Life by Hannah Wadsworth

Kelly arrived last Sunday and has settled really well in foster.  She enjoys pottering around the garden and lying in the shade - she has her favourite spots.

She has lived happily with our 4 small dogs and has been polite and friendly with all dogs she has met outside the home. She has been on two walks so far - on a longline so she can choose her pace and have a sniff in the shade - and she absolutely loves it.

In just a week she has progressed so well - she takes treats nicely and very gently and loves her food. She is accepting a fuss from us, starting to play with toys, wagging her tail and very pleased to see us when we come downstairs in the mornings.

It is great to see her look so relaxed and happy already. She is going to be a fabulous little addition to any household.

Kelly is a lovely girl who has come to us from a breeder. She would very much like to be your friend, but she is feeling a little worried and lacking in confidence at the moment.

She is still adjusting to her new surroundings and will need time, patience, and understanding to help her settle. We believe that with plenty of reassurance and gentle encouragement, Kelly will soon begin to come out of her shell and show her true personality.

Kelly will need at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home. Having a canine companion to look up to will help her learn about home life and continue to build her confidence. She can live with older dog-savvy teens who are sensible and allow her to settle in at her own pace.

As she has never lived in a home before, Kelly will need patient adopters who can help her learn all the basics, including house training and walking on a lead. To begin with, she may prefer gentle garden explorations while she gains confidence and gets used to her new environment.

Given the time, love, and support she needs, Kelly is sure to blossom into a wonderful companion. She is looking for a calm and understanding home where she can continue her journey and enjoy plenty of TLC.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Luca
description:
I'd like an active home and enjoy human company!

I’d like to be the only dog in the home. I can't live with cats or small furries!

I will need a large secure garden with minimum 6ft fencing.

I walk well on a lead and would enjoy an active lifestyle!

Please be patient with me, I haven’t lived in a home before.

I am neutered and ready to find my forever home! I have had 1 tooth extracted.

Luca is an incredible boy who has come into our care from a breeder in search of a fresh start. He already knows his name very well, so his new adopters should keep this the same to avoid confusing him as he settles into his new life.

Luca was previously used as a fake security dog and spent most of his life living outdoors, but beneath his impressive appearance is a real softie. He is easy to handle and genuinely enjoys human company, although he understandably found kennel life quite stressful when he first arrived and would often pace his kennel and the exercise yard, struggling to settle.

As Luca has become familiar with the routine, his surroundings and the staff at Many Tears, his confidence has grown enormously. One of the biggest changes we've seen is his newfound love of toys. For the first couple of weeks, he showed no interest in them at all, but now he absolutely adores them! His favourite game is a good game of fetch in the big yard, where he happily charges after his ball with great enthusiasm.

Like many large, playful dogs, Luca can occasionally become overexcited during play. He may jump up to catch the ball or become a little mouthy in his excitement, but this is never intended to cause harm. He is an incredibly gentle boy who simply gets carried away having fun. Because of his size and strength, we are helping him learn commands such as "sit", "wait" and "drop", as well as encouraging calmer play so he can enjoy himself without becoming overstimulated. This training will be important for his future adopters to continue, as an overexcited Luca could unintentionally knock over someone who is less steady on their feet.

Although he loves chasing a ball, Luca's absolute favourite toys are cuddly toys! He delights in carrying them around and showing off his softer side, which perfectly sums up his character.

Luca would be happiest as the only dog in the home, where he can enjoy all the attention, settle into a routine and build a strong bond with his new family. He will need adopters who have plenty of time to spend with him and who have experience of the breed or similar breeds. Luca cannot live with cats, small furries or small dogs.

As Luca has spent most of his life outdoors, he will need to learn about house training and the other basics of home life. With patient guidance and consistency, we are sure he will adapt well and enjoy all the comforts that a family home has to offer.

Luca already walks on a lead, although he can pull initially, so this is something his adopters will need to continue working on. He is an active, intelligent boy who would love a home where he can enjoy plenty of adventures, enrichment and quality time with his people. He is also a strong dog who can jump and even open gates, so he will need a very secure garden and confident handlers who can safely manage his strength.

Luca is an affectionate, loyal and wonderfully characterful boy who simply wants to be part of the family. Now that he has settled and his playful personality has begun to shine, it has been a joy to watch him embrace life. In the right home, with structure, ongoing training and plenty of love, Luca will make an incredibly rewarding companion.

Luca has had one tooth extracted at the time of his neuter.

Luca has been given the Gift of Life by Lynne Hughes

Before they arrive at Many Tears, many of our dogs have never had toys, and, as strange as it sounds, may not know what to do with them. Fetching, licking, and chewing are important forms of enrichment for dogs and can help to regulate their nervous system and reduce stress, but it can take a while for these dogs to figure out how to use toys in these ways. It's one of the reasons many of our dogs are recommended to have a resident dog at home, as they can learn from each other.

When Luca arrived, he did not engage with toys for the first two weeks or so. He was also very anxious, pacing his kennel and the yard and struggling to settle. However, as he has become used to the routine, surroundings, and staff at Many Tears, Luca has settled very well and discovered a love of all sorts of toys!

We try to give our dogs a mix of toys and enrichment until we know what they like. Luca is not very interested in food-based enrichment like LickiMats or Kongs. He will tug-of-war hesitantly with a rope toy but doesn't really engage with it. However, Luca LOVES a game of fetch down the big yard. He may occasionally jump up to try and get the ball and become over-excited and mouthy, so we are working on balancing his stimulation so he can get his energy out without getting worked up, and on commands like "sit", "wait", and "drop". Luca is incredibly gentle and does not want to hurt anyone, but he is a big boy and could knock a vulnerable person down if he gets too over-excited, so this kind of training is important.

Luca's FAVOURITE type of toy, though, is cuddly toys! It just proves what a big softie he is, as he really loves nothing more than carrying around his stuffed pig toy or cwtching up with his favourite, a string of stuffed Brussels sprouts. It's really lovely to see him relax and enjoy himself this way. Even better when he's doing it on your lap!

Luca is loved by all the staff and volunteers who meet him, but he's definitely ready to be someone's best friend.

Luca was previously used as a fake security dog and has spent most of his life living outside, but beneath his big appearance he is a real softie. He is easy to handle and enjoys human company, though he has understandably found kennel life quite stressful and will often plead with us to come back when we have to leave him.

Luca would be happiest and thrive best as the only dog in the home, where he can enjoy all the attention, settle more easily into a routine, and build a strong bond with his new family. He will need adopters who have plenty of time to spend with him and who have experience of the breed or similar breeds. Luca cannot live with cats or small furries and he cannot be around small dogs.

As Luca has spent most of his life outdoors, he will need to learn about house training and the other basics of home life. He will need patient adopters who can help guide him through these new experiences as he adjusts to living in a home environment.

Luca can already walk on a lead, though he does pull at first and so this is something his adopters will need to work on with him. He is a very active boy, so he would love an active household where he can enjoy plenty of adventures and become a loyal companion once he is settled in. He is a strong dog and can both jump and open gates, so he will need a very secure garden and confident handlers who are able to manage his strength safely.

Luca is an intelligent, affectionate, and loyal boy who simply wants to be close to his people. In the right home, with structure, companionship, and plenty of love, he will make an incredibly rewarding companion.

Luca has had 1 tooth extracted at the time of his neuter.

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

These wonderful souls may be older, have medical needs, or come with a few unique quirks—but they have endless love to give. Full of personality and deserving of a second chance, they come with a reduced adoption fee to help them find the perfect home. Could you be the one to give them the love they’ve been waiting for?

name: Louise
description:
I need a calm and quiet, adult only home with experienced adopters.

I will need a kind resident dog to take me under their wing. I have never met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I am not ready to learn about walking on a lead and harness yet.

I have never lived in a home so I will need help to learn house training.

I am spayed and ready to find my forever home.

Louise has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for a very special forever home. She is an extremely worried little girl who has had very little positive human socialisation and currently finds people and handling very frightening. Although she is still a very scared girl, she has already started to show us that, with patience and understanding, she is capable of making lovely progress.

Over the past month, Louise has grown in confidence every single day. While she is still terrified of being touched and picked up, she is no longer as shut down as she was when she first arrived. She is now more than happy to come over for treats and will happily sit close by while waiting for more. As she has begun to relax, her cheeky little personality has started to shine through. She likes to use her voice to let everyone know exactly how she's feeling, is very partial to chasing flies, and has quite a big personality packed into such a tiny dog. Watching her confidence slowly grow has been an absolute pleasure.

Louise will need a calm and quiet adult-only home with no visiting children, and adopters who have experience helping fearful ex-breeding dogs learn to trust. She relies heavily on the comfort and reassurance of other dogs and will need at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home who can help guide her as she settles into family life.

Although Louise takes great comfort from other dogs, she can show signs of resource guarding around food. For this reason, she will need to be fed separately from any resident dogs, and care should be taken when offering treats or other high-value food items. Her adopters will need experience managing this behaviour calmly and appropriately.

Louise is slowly becoming more comfortable with being handled, and gentle work has begun to help her get used to being picked up. While this is still very worrying for her and she can become wriggly and panic when lifted, she has never tried to bite during these sessions. We are confident this will continue to improve with time as she learns that she is safe and that nobody is going to hurt her.

Having never lived in a home before, Louise has lots to learn. Her adopters will need to be patient and understanding as she adjusts to home life and learns the basics, including house training. She is not yet ready to begin harness training or venture out into the outside world, so her future family will need to carefully plan how they will introduce these experiences when she feels ready. Everything must happen at Louise's pace to ensure she continues building confidence rather than becoming overwhelmed.

Louise is a sensitive little soul who will need a dedicated home willing to invest time, patience and understanding into helping her flourish. Every small step she takes is a huge achievement, and she continues to surprise us with just how much she is growing in confidence. With experienced adopters, kind canine companions and lots of love, we truly believe Louise will continue to blossom into the wonderful little dog we know she can become.

Louise has been given the Gift of Life by Ann Winter

Through the generosity of a Gift of Life sponsor, this dog was able to leave behind an uncertain future in Ireland and find safety and care at Many Tears. Every sponsorship helps us rescue and save more dogs in desperate need. If you would like to become part of a rescue story, please consider donating to our Gift of Life fund.

Louise has been on my section with me for about a month now, and I’ve definitely seen some positive changes in her. She’s still terrified of being touched and picked up, but her overall confidence is improving daily. She’s now more than happy to come and take treats from me and sit close to me while she’s waiting for more. She’s also nowhere near as shut down as she was when she first arrived and is now turning into quite a cheeky monkey. She does like to use her voice to express her feelings, and she’s also very partial to chasing flies! She’s actually got quite a big personality for such a little girl, and it’s a pleasure to see it emerging as the days go by.I’ve been working slowly on getting Louise used to being picked up, and while she’s definitely still very worried about it, she has calmed down slightly. She’s never tried to bite me, but she does sometimes have little panics and becomes very wriggly when she’s in my arms. I do think this is something that will improve over time. She just needs some more practice and to realise that we’re not hurting her. She obviously hasn’t been handled a lot in her life, so now it’s up to us to prove to her that it’s okay.I love working with Louise, and I can really see the potential in her. She just needs someone to give her a chance and work with her through all her worries and fears. She’s not ready for harness training or conquering the outside world yet, so that’s something her future adopters will need to take into consideration and make a plan for. I do believe she’ll progress faster in a kind home with adopters who have lots of time and patience to give her, so I really hope she won’t have to wait here for much longer.

Louise has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for a very special forever home. She is an extremely worried little girl who has had very little positive human socialisation and currently finds people and handling very frightening. At the moment, Louise feels safest keeping her distance and, if pushed beyond her comfort zone, may make the wrong decision and nip purely out of fear. She is a very scared girl who needs understanding, patience and time.Louise will need a calm and quiet adult-only home with no visiting children, and adopters who have experience of helping fearful ex-breeding dogs learn to trust. She relies heavily on the comfort and reassurance of other dogs and will need at least one other kind and confident resident dog in her new home who can take her under their wing.Although Louise takes great comfort from other dogs, she can show signs of resource guarding around food. For this reason, she will need to be fed separately from other dogs, and care will need to be taken when giving treats or other high-value food items. Her adopters will need experience of managing this behaviour and the confidence to do so appropriately.Having never lived in a home before, Louise has lots to learn. Her adopters will need to be patient and understanding as she adjusts to home life and learns new skills such as house training. She is not yet ready to begin learning about lead walking or wearing a harness, as handling is still very worrying for her. These things will need to be introduced gradually and at her pace once she feels more comfortable and secure.Louise is a sensitive soul who will need a dedicated home willing to invest time, patience and understanding into helping her build confidence. We will continue to work with her every day, helping her take small steps forward at a pace she is comfortable with. In the right home, with the support of canine friends and experienced adopters, we hope Louise will slowly learn that people can be kind and that life has so much to offer.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Maggie
description:
I'd like a quiet, adult-only home to help me relax and feel safe.

I can be an only dog or live with one dog my size or larger.

I'd like a secure garden to play in that has 6ft fencing.

I walk well on a lead and would enjoy one or two walks a day!

I have lived in a home so I know the basics.

I have been spayed and I’m ready for my new home! Read below for more!

Maggie is a sweet girl who originally came to us from a breeder. She was adopted for around 16 months, but has sadly returned to us from a home following an incident with one of the smaller resident dogs in the home. During the evening, while out in the garden, Maggie was frightened by the other dog barking. Although we don't know exactly what happened, the interaction unfortunately ended in a fight and they struggled to make up or co-exist after this.

When Maggie returned to our care, she was a very shy and sensitive girl who was quite worried about meeting new people. Since then she has settled in wonderfully and has well and truly come out of her shell! We would absolutely love to see her back in a home as soon as possible.

Maggie is now confident enough that we would consider homing her as an only dog in the right circumstances, with adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs and who have plenty of time to spend with her and to help her settle in. She could also live with another calm and confident resident dog, but they would need to be her size or larger.

As Maggie has previously lived in a home, she will be familiar with the sights and sounds of a home environment. However, she may still need some support with house training as she settles into a new routine. Maggie can already walk on a lead and will enjoy going for walks with her new family.

Maggie can be particularly worried by men and did initially bark at the male resident in her previous home. With time, patience, and gentle handling, she slowly began to come around. We are ensuring she socialises with males here and she does have a male staff member as part of her care team and has bonded well with him, which is lovely.

Maggie will be looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle in at her own pace. Unfortunately on one occasion, Maggie became tangled in her harness and in a panic, bit the hand of the male owner who was trying to help free her. This was purely through fear, but due to this, Maggie will be looking for an adult only home.

Since Maggie has been with us, she has shown that when she feels stressed or overwhelmed, she may try to find a way to escape by climbing. Because of this, she will need a very secure garden with boundaries of at least 6ft to help keep her safe and prevent any potential escape attempts.

Once Maggie knows and trusts someone, she is a very sweet and happy girl. In the right calm and understanding home, with patient and dog-savvy owners who are willing to let her settle at her own pace, we are confident she will continue to make wonderful progress and become the most lovely companion.

Maggie is currently being treated for an ear infection in the left ear.

Maggie has settled into rescue life beautifully, but having spent over a year in a home, we are really desperate to get her back into one ASAP!

Since her arrival, Maggie has been kenneled with our lovely Bocker Eddie whom she gets along with wonderfully. She does enjoy her time with him, and would happily live alongside one resident dog - male or female, her size or larger!!

During Maggie’s time here she has formed a wonderful bond with her kennel mums who she absolutely dotes on. She’s even bonded with a kennel dad who has said she’s an absolute sweetheart, and he’s had no trouble with her too, which is so good to hear.

Maggie's kennel mummies describe her as loyal, affectionate, and loving. She really looks to them for reassurance, and when they’re around nothing else matters. Seeing as she’s grown so much in confidence, we’re happy to say she can now also be homed as an only dog too in the circumstance her adopters have lots of time to spend with her!!

Maggie isn’t really too fussed on playing with toys, but what she does love is her food & treats so gaining her trust will be a breeze!

A dog like Maggie is absolutely wasted in kennels, she’s already lived a life at home & we are so desperate to get her back into one!!

Maggie is a sweet Cockapoo who originally came to us from a breeder. She was adopted for around 16 months, but has sadly returned to us from a home following an incident with one of the smaller resident dogs in the home. During the evening, while out in the garden, Maggie was frightened by the other dog barking. Although we don't know exactly what happened, the interaction unfortunately ended in a fight and they struggled to make up or co-exist after this.

Maggie is a very shy and sensitive girl who is wary of strangers, although she does allow handling. She is trying very hard to be brave and is beginning to show small signs of progress, even getting close to taking treats from our hands, though she is not quite confident enough just yet.

Maggie is not yet confident enough to be an only dog, so we feel she would settle best in a home with one calm and confident resident dog of a similar size or larger, who can help her feel safe and reassured as she adjusts.

As Maggie has previously lived in a home, she will be familiar with the sights and sounds of a home environment. However, she may still need some support with house training as she settles into a new routine. Maggie can already walk on a lead, so she would enjoy one or two walks around the local area with the resident dog when she is settled in.

Maggie is particularly worried by men and did initially bark at the male resident in her previous home. With time, patience, and gentle handling, she slowly began to come around. Unfortunately, there was also an incident where Maggie became tangled in her harness and, in a panic, bit the hand of the male owner who was trying to help free her. Because of this, Maggie will need a calm adult-only home with patient and understanding adopters who have experience or understanding of the challenges that can come with rehoming ex-breeding dogs.

Once Maggie knows and trusts someone, she is a very sweet and happy girl. In the right calm and understanding home, with patient and dog-savvy owners who are willing to let her settle at her own pace, we are confident she will continue to make wonderful progress and slowly come out of her shell.

This dog can be homed with a resident dog.

name: Lady
description:
I would like a calm and quiet home where I can settle in at my own pace.

I need another kind resident dog in my new home. I haven't met cats.

I would like a secure garden to spend time in.

I’ve never been on walks before and will need a gentle introduction.

I am in a foster home now so am learning all about home life and house training.

I have had 1 tooth extracted and am ready to find my forever home!

Lady has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for her forever home. She is a sweet and gentle girl who was a little worried to find herself here when she first arrived, but did start to soon become very inquisitive and was trying so hard to be brave. She has since moved to a wonderful foster home where she is settling in well and learning all about home life for the first time ever.

Lady is looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle at her own pace and build her confidence, she would benefit from adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs. Her confidence is starting to grow and she will now allow her fosterer to gently stroke her and seems to enjoy this.

She takes great comfort from the company of other dogs, so she will need at least one kind and confident resident dog in her new home to help her feel secure as she settles in. She can guard her food bowl from other dogs, so she will need to be feed separately in her new home and will need adopters who are confident managing this, but she hasn't shown any signs of guarding treats.

Lady had never lived in a home before moving to foster, she is starting to learn all about home life and house training and is toileting on newspaper at the moment, so her adopters will need to be kind and understanding as she continues to learn. She doesn't yet walk on a lead and harness and will need to practice first around the home and garden before venturing further.

With time, kindness and encouragement, we believe Lady will blossom into a wonderful companion. Lady is a truly sweet girl, and we can’t wait to see her thrive in a loving home.

She has had one tooth extracted at the time of her spay.

Lady has been given the Gift of Life by Lynne Hughes

Lady has now been in her foster home for just over a week and is settling in well. She is a gentle, sweet girl who will now stand for gentle strokes and, after just a few days, was comfortable enough to allow her foster carer to clean and trim around her eyes. She is also doing well with her house training and will use newspapers when indoors.

Lady won't take long to settle into a forever home, as long as she's given the time and space to get to know her new family and surroundings. Once she feels safe, her lovely personality really begins to shine.

Lady has come to us from a breeder and is now looking for her forever home. She is a sweet and gentle girl who is a little worried to find herself here, but she has been easy to handle and is already becoming a very inquisitive little girl! It's clear that she is trying so hard to be brave and really wants to come and say hello, so we don't think it will take her too long to come out of her shell in her new home.

Lady is looking for a calm and quiet home where she can settle at her own pace and build her confidence, she would benefit from adopters who have experience with ex-breeding dogs. We would consider homing her with gentle dog savvy older teenagers.

Lady has never lived in a home before, so she will need patient and understanding adopters who can help her learn all about home life. She will need support with the basics such as house training, learning to walk on a lead, and discovering that the world isn’t such a scary place. With time, kindness and encouragement, we believe Lady will blossom into a wonderful companion.
